item.description: Did you think about hitting the Dougie when you guys won? John Wall: No, I retired it. I retired it. I retired it.

item.title: Ainge also told me he would consider trading down from …
item.description: Ainge also told me he would consider trading down from Utah's top selection. "We're open," he said. "We'll always listen."

item.title: Pritchard said himself, later, that he views Zubac as …
item.description: Don't take my word for it. Pritchard said himself, later, that he views Zubac as his team's lottery pick (to an extent) when reflecting on the trade. "At the trade deadline, didn't know how structurally we were (going to be) able to get a top center. Then you might be just looking at free agency and using your cap. And we wouldn't have cap space. So it's a full mid level. And that guy usually isn't in the level of (Zubac)," he began before the kicker. "So (Zubac) is our lottery pick."

item.title: I'm told that the league will very much consider …
item.description: If the NBA's ballyhooed new "3-2-1" format is indeed voted in on May 28 — with approval from 23 of the league's 30 teams required to enact the changes — there would be 37 Ping Pong balls total for the 16 teams that participate in the lottery … all adorned with a team logo. This would replace the current system that features 14 numbered Ping Pong balls with 1,001 possible combinations divided up amongst the 14 lottery teams. I'm told that the league will very much consider holding that drawing li

item.title: Terance Mann: Bruh
“Draymond Green is the first player to make four …
item.description: Shane Gillis to Draymond Green during the Kevin Hart roasting: “Draymond Green is the first player to make four All-Star teams for just setting picks.”

item.title: Anthony Edwards explodes for 36 as Wolves even series vs. Spurs
item.description: Anthony Slater: Timberwolves survive a shaky final minute, take advantage of the Victor Wembanyama second quarter ejection, seal Game 4 with a handful of makes at the rim in the clutch. Anthony Edwards had 36 points in 40 minutes. Series tied 2-2 going back to San Antonio.

item.title: The NBA will review Victor Wembanyama’s flagrant-2 …
item.description: To clarify: The NBA will review Victor Wembanyama’s flagrant-2 foul tomorrow. It is standard operating procedure for all flagrant fouls. League discipline czar James Jones has discretion on whether more punishment (fine or suspension) is warranted.

item.title: That flagrant foul 2 on Victor Wembanyama marks his …
item.description: Alan Horton: That flagrant foul 2 on Victor Wembanyama marks his 1st career ejection

item.title: Tyrese Maxey said he will debrief the season …
item.description: Gina Mizell: Tyrese Maxey said he will debrief the season by...becoming a new dad. You know this if you're a very online Sixers/NBA fan, but it's really the first time he's addressed in this type of setting. "I’m ready to go home and then just see (what) that’s like, that’s just honestly the facts of it."

item.title: Paul George was asked what he's focusing on this …
item.description: Derek Bodner: Paul George was asked what he's focusing on this summer. "Figuring out if I can get that explosiveness back. It limited me a lot this year. My entire career [was] the jump shot kinda being the jab...I just felt like that [explosiveness] wasn't there this year."

item.title: Luka Doncic (left hamstring strain) remains out for …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Luka Doncic (left hamstring strain) remains out for Game 4 against OKC tomorrow

item.title: Congrats to these 5 standouts from the 2026 AWS NBA G …
item.description: NBA G League: Congrats to these 5 standouts from the 2026 AWS NBA G League Draft Combine. They'll continue on to this week’s 2026 AWS NBA Draft Combine. 🏀 Rafael Castro 🏀 Jacob Cofie 🏀 Bryce Hopkins 🏀 Trey Kaufman-Renn 🏀 Aaron Nkrumah

item.title: Victor Wembanyama ejected after throwing elbow at Naz Reid
item.description: Anthony Slater: Flagrant 2 on Victor Wembanyama for a swinging elbow to Naz Reid’s chin. He’s ejected early second quarter. Potential to swing the series.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama elbows Naz Reid in the face, he was …
item.description: CJ Fogler: Victor Wembanyama elbows Naz Reid in the face, he was given a flagrant 2 and ejected

item.title: NBA Last Two Minute Report from Game 3 shows 0 errors. …
item.description: Chris Fedor: NBA Last Two Minute Report from Game 3 shows 0 errors. Tobias Harris’ late contest on James Harden’s 3-point dagger was clean, not considered a foul. “Harris jumps to contest Harden's shot, but would otherwise avoid making contact if not for Harden extending forward.”

item.title: Jalen Williams (left hamstring strain) remains out for …
item.description: Justin Martinez: OKC's Jalen Williams (left hamstring strain) remains out for Game 4 against the Los Angeles Lakers tomorrow.

item.title: Daryl Morey is on his way to Chicago for the draft …
item.description: Austin Krell: For those wondering, Daryl Morey is on his way to Chicago for the draft combine. He will not speak tonight or tomorrow.

item.title: Tyrese Maxey on Knicks fans taking over the 76ers arena: ‘It absolutely sucks’
item.description: Derek Bodner: Tyrese Maxey on the Knicks fan takeover in Philly: "It absolutely sucks. It just sucks. That's really all I can say about it, man...There's only one way to put a stop to it: we have to go out there and win these games. It felt louder here for them than it did in the Garden."

item.title: Tyrese Maxey, on VJ Edgecombe's growth into being more …
item.description: Derek Bodner: Tyrese Maxey, on VJ Edgecombe's growth into being more of a perimeter initiator: "I think next year I'm gonna do some things off the ball, and I don't have to be in front of the defense all the time. When you're in front of the defense, it gives them an opportunity to trap you."

item.title: VJ Edgecombe says he does not know where he is …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: VJ Edgecombe says he does not know where he is spending his summer. He has been “on the road for the past six years.” He thinks he’ll spend time in The Bahamas, New York and Texas, where he can work out with Tyrese Maxey.

item.title: “Next year, I’m going to come back better, stronger …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: “Next year, I’m going to come back better, stronger faster, more athletic,” VJ Edgecombe said. “Whatever I got to do, I’m going to go.”

item.title: Kelly Oubre on free agency: 'I've averaged 20 points in this league and still find myself barely getting any contracts'
item.description: Derek Bodner: Kelly Oubre Jr on free agency: "I've averaged 20 points in this league and still find myself barely getting any contracts...I hope I did myself a good service by being more efficient, slowing down, and playing better overall basketball." Oubre says he loves it in Philly.

item.title: Asked Kelly Oubre Jr. about what he is prioritizing as …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Asked Kelly Oubre Jr. about what he is prioritizing as far as situation is concerned. He said he wants to be “somewhere I’m loved,” then spoke at length about how, as a husband and father of two kids, he wants his children to have a place they can call home.

item.title: VJ Edgecombe, on Joel Embiid's praise: "I really love …
item.description: Derek Bodner: VJ Edgecombe, on Joel Embiid's praise: "I really love my teammates and I really love all the good things they say about me, but I gotta put in the work to continue getting better so what they say can come to pass.'"

item.title: Tyrese Maxey says his finger got a bit better after …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Tyrese Maxey says his finger got a bit better after the Orlando game. At the end of the second quarter of Game 2 in Boston, he got it caught in a jersey and fell on it. “It hurts bad.” It happened earlier vs. Detroit, too. He’ll do hand therapy over the summer.

item.title: Wolves start Ayo Dosunmu over Mike Conley in Game 4 vs. Spurs
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Chris Finch putting Ayo Dosunmu in the starting lineup tonight for the first time in these playoffs. Bet you'll see Shannon as the featured ball-handler with the second unit.

item.title: Quentin Grimes says he has not yet thought about his …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Quentin Grimes says he has not yet thought about his unrestricted free agency this summer. Called his season “solid.”

item.title: Joel Embiid, asked about whether he thinks about his …
item.description: Derek Bodner: Joel Embiid, asked about whether he thinks about his legacy: "I want to win more than anyone. It sucks to lose. I haven't won anything, so that hurts. But to go home and raise [his son], raise my daughter, look at my wife in the eyes and understand I'm a good man...Really, that's all that matters."

item.title: Joel Embiid, on the Knicks: "At times, it's okay to …
item.description: Derek Bodner: Joel Embiid, on the Knicks: "At times, it's okay to just say the other team was better....Gotta get better, from top to bottom. Ownership, front office, players, coaches. Everybody just gotta get better."

item.title: Miles McBride on reaching back-to-back Eastern …
item.description: Ian Begley: Miles McBride on reaching back-to-back Eastern Conference Finals: “That’s what’s expected… that’s not the goal.”

item.title: The Knicks are the 4th team in NBA history with …
item.description: ESPN Insights: The Knicks are the 4th team in NBA history with multiple 30-point series-clinching wins in a single postseason. They join the 2025 Thunder, 2008 Celtics, and 1987 Lakers. All 3 of those teams went on to win the NBA title.

item.title: Joel Embiid on his knee issues before coming into the season: 'I thought I was done'
item.description: Kyle Neubeck: Joel Embiid says that the success they had managing his knee this year made it a success for him despite today's season-ending loss: "We came into the season thinking there was not much left… I thought I was done"

item.title: Joel Embiid says he had complications after his …
item.description: Derek Bodner: Joel Embiid says he had complications after his appendectomy. "The things that I've been dealing with, they've all been related to the surgery. Coming back early, the core was weak, everything was affected. So you're looking at the hip, the adductor, everything is out of place."

item.title: Joel Embiid says he wants to spend his whole career in the 76ers: 'I love Philly'
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Asked about his relationship with the Sixers’ front office, Joel Embiid said “I love Philly,” reiterated his desire to win in Philadelphia and spend his whole career here, and talked about the disappointment of not winning a title. Did not address where he is at with management.

item.title: Joel Embiid with high praise for rookie VJ Edgecombe: …
item.description: Kyle Neubeck: Joel Embiid with high praise for rookie VJ Edgecombe: "That guy is something different, and this was only year one…he has a chance to be extremely special" and hopes to help him continue developing in the months and years ahead

item.title: Nick Nurse, on VJ Edgecombe's rookie season: "That was …
item.description: Derek Bodner: Nick Nurse, on VJ Edgecombe's rookie season: "That was a hell of an impressive rookie season. He's a hell of a player. It's not about shooting, scoring, jumping, all that stuff. He really knows how to play. He really has a leadership quality to him. I think he's a bigtime winner."

item.title: Nick Nurse on Joel Embiid: 'He worked his a** off to get out there and play'
item.description: Derek Bodner: Nick Nurse, on Joel Embiid: "He worked his ass off to get out there and play. I think it was really difficult for him, especially [in Game 3]. He gave us everything he could. I commend him."

item.title: Nick Nurse, on New York's open 3s: "I think we came …
item.description: Derek Bodner: Nick Nurse, on New York's open 3s: "I think we came out ready to go, we executed well on the offensive end and shots didn't do in, and it bothered our guys. We just couldn't get over that. I think that dispirited them at the start."

item.title: Rui Hachimura on the energy in today's practice, with …
item.description: Khobi Price: Rui Hachimura on the energy in today's practice, with the Lakers aware of the challenge they have being down 0-3 to the Thunder: "It's tough. They are championship [team] for a reason. We focus on a lot on Shai but I think we talk about more...we should have talked about more other guys. They're killing us. Especially when Shai is off the court, we're really losing those minutes. We talked about that today, how we're gonna guard them as a team. We feel good about it tomorrow."

item.title: Mitch Johnson on Spurs: 'I don’t think we’re anywhere near being a finished product by any means'
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Mitch Johnson with reason for rest of NBA to worry: “We have a lot of young players I don’t think are anywhere near reaching their ceiling...And we’re still learning about each other. The coach has room to grow and get better. I don’t think we’re anywhere near being a finished product by any means”

item.title: Should there be a Game 5 the Lakers vs. Thunder will …
item.description: Rylan Stiles: Should there be a Game 5 the Lakers vs. Thunder will tip off at 8:30 PM CT Wednesday.

item.title: "We’ve got a guy who makes it easy for us. His name is …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Masai Ujiri: "We’ve got a guy who makes it easy for us. His name is Cooper Flagg. Wherever you go around the world, that’s the gem you’re looking for. That’s the type of guy you’re looking for."

item.title: LaMelo Ball and his partner Ana Montana are officially …
item.description: LaMelo Ball and his partner Ana Montana are officially parents. The NBA star, 24, and the model, whose real name is Analicia Chaves, have welcomed their first baby together, son LaOne, a rep for the couple confirms to PEOPLE exclusively. Ball and Montana, who welcomed their son this past January, are also launching I Am Fertility, a platform and nonprofit dedicated to supporting women navigating fertility and IVF journeys.

item.title: Knicks sweep Philadelphia in lights-out shooting game
item.description: Tim Bontemps: Final: Knicks 144, Sixers 114 An absolute demolition by the Knicks, who sweep Philly, pick up a seventh consecutive victory overall & advance to the Eastern Conference finals for a second straight year. Philly has not advanced to the conference finals for a quarter century.

item.title: Knicks just tied the NBA playoff record for most …
item.description: Stefan Bondy: Knicks just tied the NBA playoff record for most 3-pointers in a game.

item.title: Best Knicks playoff series ever in point differential: …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Best Knicks playoff series ever in point differential: 2026 vs. Atlanta: +105. 2026 vs. Philadelphia: +89. Yes, their two best marks in NBA history have happened in this postseason

item.title: Knicks will sweep a best-of-7 series for the first …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Knicks will sweep a best-of-7 series for the first time since 1999 & the third time in team history. Last time the Knicks swept a series, they went to the Finals...

item.title: The Knicks will now have at least a week off until the …
item.description: Adam Zagoria: The Knicks will now have at least a week off until the conference finals. The earliest they would play Game 1 would be next Sunday, per Mike Breen.

item.title: Caris LeVert listed as questionable for Game 4 vs. Cavs
item.description: The Pistons have downgraded Caris LeVert (right heel contusion) to questionable for Monday’s Game 4 against Cleveland. Kevin Huerter (left adductor strain) is also questionable.

item.title: Knicks with 18 threes in the first half, tied for the …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Knicks with 18 threes in the first half, tied for the most in a playoff half in NBA history. Most since the Cavs in 2016.

item.title: Khobi Price: JJ Redick when asked about this sequence …
item.description: Khobi Price: JJ Redick when asked about this sequence from Deandre Ayton: "I’ve been around DA for a season now. I know how special he is for our group to succeed. And I told him this morning, I said to him in the Houston series at one point he was the big part of the reason we were winning the series. He was a big part of the reason we won the series. I believe in him and he’s going to help us win tomorrow."

item.title: Bryson Graham on moving from No. 9 to 4: "I'm in the …
item.description: K.C. Johnson: Bryson Graham on moving from No. 9 to 4: "I'm in the thought of it doesn't matter where we pick, we're going to get the best player. But the odds working in your favor today does help."

item.title: Pacers president Kevin Prichard apologies to fans after losing first-round pick: 'I own taking this risk'
item.description: Kevin Pritchard: I'm really sorry to all our fans. I own taking this risk. Surprised it came up 5th after this year. I thought we were due some luck. But please remember - this team deserved a starting center to compete with the best teams next year. We have always been resilient.

item.title: “I mean, just thinking about it, I prayed for moments …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: “I mean, just thinking about it, I prayed for moments like these and worked for moments like these, so just to get that opportunity has been special and go out there and do what I can for the guys” - Adou Thiero on going from out of the rotation to grabbing 8 reb in 13 mins in G3

item.title: Knicks fans heckiling Joel Embiid at the free throw …
item.description: CJ Fogler: Knicks fans heckiling Joel Embiid at the free throw line in Philly

item.title: Obviously, I’m not saying that we’re perfect or that …
item.description: Nick Wright got a lot of exposure for calling LaMelo Ball a clown, specifically. He said he’s unserious, and LaMelo has had that “unserious” label for quite a few years. I’d say this year is the first year where people started to go, “Oh, wait a minute.” What’s it like to hear your guard talked about so negatively in the press? Do you hear it, and do you think anything of it? Moussa Diabaté: I mean, it’s one of those things where most people are not in the locker room. Everybody is going to hav

item.title: Knicks tie playoff record for most three-pointers in a quarter
item.description: ESPN Insights: The Knicks are rolling early. They have tied the NBA playoff record for most 3-pt FG in any quarter (11).

item.title: Mark Daigneault on his relationship with the team: “I …
item.description: Clemente Almanza: Mark Daigneault on his relationship with the team: “I got great advice one time. You need to be there when they need you. Early on, when we were younger, I think they needed us a little bit more in terms of direction, in terms of guidance, in terms of some of the stuff we're talking about. As time's gone on, there's been situations where they've needed us less. I think the worst thing you can do in those situations is stifle their autonomy and over-coach their ownership.”

item.title: "I mean, being on the Lakers, you feel like your …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: "I mean, being on the Lakers, you feel like your back’s against the wall every game. So I think the whole season has prepared us to be in a position where we're in …” - JJ Redick on if the adversity LAL has faced this season preps them for this OKC challenge

item.title: Sean Marks on if #Nets falling to 6th in the draft …
item.description: Brian Lewis: Sean Marks on if #Nets falling to 6th in the draft will make them more active in trades: “Hard to tell. It’s all about how these guys develop. I don’t think you want to make rash decisions before you’ve seen how they look.”

item.title: Milwaukee Bucks GM Jon Horst says trading or keeping Giannis Antetokounmpo is still on the table
item.description: Jon Horst: Just got out of the NBA Draft lottery drawing room, and talking to #Bucks GM Jon Horst. The "two paths" of trading/keeping Giannis Antetokounmpo are obviously both still on the table. Horst was happy the team didn't fall in the draft, which was more probable than moving up.

item.title: Informative media session with Bryson Graham just now. …
item.description: K.C. Johnson: Informative media session with Bryson Graham just now. Some takeaways: Always draft best player available over positional need. Let draft process play out with open mind (“Although you may feel confident about a certain player, don’t dig your heels in yet”)

item.title: The Pacers aren't done paying for Zubac btw. The LA …
item.description: Law Murray: The Pacers aren't done paying for Zubac btw. The LA Clippers will also receive the 2028 second round Pacers pick AND The 2029 Pacers first round pick. Bennedict Mathurin is a restricted free agent and Isaiah Jackson is under contract through 2028

item.title: The Pacers keep their 2031 first. That was only going …
item.description: Law Murray: The Pacers keep their 2031 first. That was only going to the Clippers if the Clippers did not get the 2026 first, which they have now.

item.title: Bulls EVP Bryson Graham: 'I just got the job and I got the 4th pick, this is crazy'
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: "I can't believe it," Bulls EVP of basketball ops Bryson Graham said. "I just got the job and I got the 4th pick. This is crazy."

item.title: This crowd is overwhelmingly -- I mean overwhemingly …
item.description: Stefan Bondy: This crowd is overwhelmingly -- I mean overwhemingly -- Knicks fans. It's really like a game at MSG.

item.title: 4-2-1-13 combination gives Wizards the No. 1 pick in the 2026 draft
item.description: NBA Communications: The winning combination of ping-pong balls that earned Washington the #1 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft: 4, 2, 1,13. Watch the full lottery drawing below.

item.title: The team with the worst record in the NBA has won the …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: The team with the worst record in the NBA has won the draft lottery for the first time since the NBA changed the format in 2019.

item.title: Utah actually won the No. 3 pick after popping up …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Emerging from the sequestered lottery room to report Utah actually won the No. 3 pick after popping up second as well. So, the league had to toss out that outcome and draw again… which led to the Grizzlies claiming the third pick.

item.title: 2019: Pelicans trade Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: 2019: Pelicans trade Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft. 2025: Mavericks trade for Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft. 2026: Wizards trade for Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft

item.title: Wizards win 2026 draft lottery
item.description: Shams Charania: Top 4 order in the 2026 NBA Draft: 1. Washington Wizards 2. Utah Jazz 3. Memphis Grizzlies 4. Chicago Bulls

item.title: Washington Wizards: JUST FELL TO OUR KNEES IN THE …
item.description: Washington Wizards: JUST FELL TO OUR KNEES IN THE MIDDLE OF CAPITAL 𝙊𝙉𝙀 ARENA

item.title: Wizards, Jazz, Grizzlies and Bulls make the top four in the draft lottery
item.description: Josh Robbins: The Wizards, Jazz, Grizzlies and Bulls are the teams that will draft somewhere in the top four.

item.title: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft …
item.description: NBA: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft Lottery 2026: 5. LA Clippers 6. Brooklyn Nets 7. Sacramento Kings 8. Atlanta Hawks 9. Dallas Mavericks 10. Milwaukee Bucks 11. Golden State Warriors 12. Oklahoma City Thunder 13. Miami Heat 14. Charlotte Hornets

item.title: The LA Clippers have STOLEN the Indiana Pacers pick …
item.description: Law Murray: The LA Clippers have STOLEN the Indiana Pacers pick and will draft FIFTH

item.title: OG Anunoby out vs. Sixers
item.description: The Knicks are playing it cautiously. OG Anunoby (right hamstring strain) is out for their Game 4 clash against the 76ers Sunday afternoon at Xfinity Mobile Arena as they look to finish off a series sweep. He also missed the Knicks’ Game 3 win on Friday with the injury. He is still considered day-to-day and the Knicks have been optimistic that he won’t miss much time. Imaging revealed that it is a “very minor” strain, The Post’s Stefan Bondy previously reported.

item.title: Brown shares that OG Anunoby is out for today's Game 4 …
item.description: Knicks Videos: Mike Brown shares that OG Anunoby is out for today's Game 4 matchup with the 76ers:

item.title: Deandre Ayton’s signature soft touch around the basket …
item.description: Deandre Ayton’s signature soft touch around the basket has suddenly escaped him. After shooting 60.4% from the field during the first round and a career-best 67.1% during the regular season, Ayton is shooting 39.3% (11 for 28) against the Thunder. He made just three of 11 shots in the restricted area during the first two games. The Lakers needed Ayton to thrive during this series against the double-big lineup of Chet Holmgren and Isaiah Hartenstein. Some of Ayton’s shooting struggles have depend

item.title: Austin Reaves, sigh, has clearly been unable to …
item.description: Austin Reaves, sigh, has clearly been unable to overcome his oblique injury and playoff demons. He was lousy again Saturday with five baskets and five turnovers and a bunch of guff for the officials. This left the major hauling to James, who’s not officially hurt, but here’s guessing his whole body is hurting. He’s not officially exhausted, but here’s guessing his whole body is tired. He’s not officially a senior citizen, but in NBA terms he’s definitely a senior citizen and during the most impo

item.title: If the Warriors do somehow get their hands on another …
item.description: Another reason to trust Kerr: If the Warriors do somehow get their hands on another star — and you can bet a Lacob-run franchise will try — then Golden State will require Kerr’s greatest strength of maximizing excellence. In the alternate universe where the Warriors land Giannis Antetokounmpo or Kawhi Leonard or LeBron James, Kerr is the coach you’d want. Go watch “Court of Gold” on Netflix and see why.

item.title: Pratt was spotted in the fourth quarter sitting in …
item.description: Los Angeles mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt made an appearance at Game 3 of the Western Conference Semifinals between the Oklahoma City Thunder and the Lakers on Saturday night at Crypto.com Arena. Pratt was spotted in the fourth quarter sitting in former owner Jeanie Buss’ second-row seats behind the court.

item.title: Brandon Clarke dead at 29 years old
item.description: Michael Scotto: Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke has passed away at 29 years old. Clarke was a first-round draft pick in 2019 and was an All-Rookie First Team selection. He averaged 10.2 points and 5.5 rebounds in seven NBA seasons for Memphis. Thoughts and prayers to his loved ones 🙏🏼

item.title: Statement on the passing of Brandon Clarke
item.description: Priority Sports: Statement on the passing of Brandon Clarke.

item.title: During this difficult time, the family kindly asks for …
item.description: Priority Sports: During this difficult time, the family kindly asks for privacy as they grieve and make arrangements. Your thoughts, prayers, and understanding are greatly appreciated.

item.title: Grizzlies release statement on tragic passing of Brandon Clarke
item.description: Memphis Grizzlies: Another statement on the passing of Brandon Clarke.

item.title: "I wish we had more time with those guys all in the …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: JJ Redick on the big 3: "I wish we had more time with those guys all in the lineup. We were just beginning to see what that could look like when Luka and AR got hurt and that's sort of the disappointing part because I think we all felt that excitement and that belief that we were beginning to be a championship level team and would have loved to have seen that group that was finishing the regular season be able to play healthy in the playoffs"

item.title: "I've always had to play in a pair of Hanes crew socks …
item.description: JOHN WALL WAS sitting at the Adorn Bar in the Four Seasons. It was Sunday morning, and the No. 1 selection in the 2010 draft was picking at the bacon on his plate and explaining his superstitions: They were underneath his crisp navy blue and red Howard University Air Jordan 14 exclusives, he said. "I've always had to play in a pair of Hanes crew socks from Walmart, under my NBA socks," Wall told ESPN. "And they got to be ultra cushioned. I need them."

item.title: "Did you see that?" Wall asked, astounded at the …
item.description: Wall was at the restaurant in downtown Chicago representing his former team, the Washington Wizards, at perhaps the most pivotal point they've had in a generation -- since he arrived as a prized prospect 16 years ago. In between bites, Wall described his beloved good luck charm: a necklace his late mother wore. He last had it on Jan. 29, the night the Wizards celebrated his career. The clamp broke, however. Fortunately, he has a backup: a tattoo on his neck of his mother wearing the necklace. Ju

item.title: "This one feels like it's a lot more like, 'We need to …
item.description: ESPN was granted exclusive behind-the-scenes access to the Wizards throughout the draft lottery weekend, from Wall to their front office, seeking to capture a full view of an organization at its most tenuous time. Either they'd fall, yet again, delaying once more the NBA's most arduous rebuild, or they'd strike lottery gold and break in an instant a spell of misfortune that had lasted for decades. "This one feels like it's a lot more like, 'We need to do this!'" said Wall, who wanted a better re

item.title: "[Sunday,] I feel, was an incredible day of luck," …
item.description: Sunday's lottery luck provides the potential for a new, foundational pillar for a franchise used to disaster always being around the corner. "[Sunday,] I feel, was an incredible day of luck," Leonsis said. "If you watch 'Final Destination,' that's all I could think of was, 'Here I am. I'm going to end up in the hospital. I will have lost an eye and we'll pick fifth."

item.title: "I would say the Wizards can't have a bad day today."
item.description: The Wizards went into the day knowing they couldn't drop lower than fifth and were confident this draft -- headlined by college stars such as Kansas' Darryn Peterson, Duke's Cameron Boozer, North Carolina's Caleb Wilson and Arkansas' Darius Acuff Jr. -- would yield at least five players with All-Star ceilings. ESPN draft analyst Jeremy Woo projects BYU's AJ Dybantsa to be selected by Washington. "This draft is pretty deep," Dawkins said before the lottery started. "To know you can't fall further

item.title: Ayo Dosunmu jokes about what the Timberwolves’ mindset …
item.description: Chris Hine: Ayo Dosunmu jokes about what the Timberwolves’ mindset is tonight ahead of Game 5: “We coming out, we sending a message — nah, I’m just playing.” Later added that the Wolves were expecting Victor Wembanyama to not get suspended and play tonight.

item.title: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver issued the following …

item.title: Brandon Clarke's death being investigated as possible drug overdose
item.description: Brandon Clarke's death is being investigated as a possible overdose ... with officials finding drug paraphernalia inside the home the Memphis Grizzlies player was staying at on Monday. The Los Angeles Fire Department responded to a medical emergency call around 5 PM on Monday ... and when paramedics got to the scene, Clarke was declared deceased, according to NBC4.

item.title: In 2019 we headed to Summer League, and the kid out of …
item.description: Chris Vernon: in 2019 we headed to Summer League, and the kid out of Gonzaga they drafted 21st overall won the MVP. He became beloved from literally the first time he wore the uniform. Look up and 7 years later he is gone. Still can’t believe it. So intensely sad. RIP BC 😢

item.title: Jason Collins (1978-2026)
item.description: Shams Charania: 13-year NBA veteran Jason Collins has died at 47 years old after a battle with Stage 4 glioblastoma. Collins was the first active, openly gay player in NBA history. RIP.

item.title: The NBA concluded its investigation into the Milwaukee …
item.description: The NBA concluded its investigation into the Milwaukee Bucks over the shutdown of Giannis Antetokounmpo and has determined that no further action is warranted, sources told ESPN's Shams Charania on Tuesday. The league launched its probe early last month after Antetokounmpo and the Bucks told different stories about his ability to return to the court. Antetokounmpo hyperextended his left knee on March 15 and did not appear again for the Bucks during the regular season.

item.title: The Bucks told investigators that Antetokounmpo …
item.description: Antetokounmpo said multiple times that he was healthy enough to return before the end of the season but that the team would not medically clear him. Milwaukee, meanwhile, informed the league that it didn't believe Antetokounmpo wanted to actually play. The Bucks told investigators that Antetokounmpo declined the opportunity to play 3-on-3 scrimmages as part of the return protocol from his knee injury. Antetokounmpo told the league that he was healthy enough to return to action regardless.

item.title: De’Aaron Fox (sore ankle), Dylan Harper (sore knee) …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: De’Aaron Fox (sore ankle), Dylan Harper (sore knee) will be game-time decisions, per Mitch Johnson. "I know they're fighting, so if they can, they'll suit up," the coach said. "But got to make sure we save them from themselves also and make sure that they're capable of being out there."

item.description: Alan Horton: NBA has announced Friday's Game 6 at Target Center will be an 8:30 PM CT start -- technically an 8:42 tip with a possible 5-minute slide to 8:47.

item.description: Shams Charania: The NBA concluded its investigation into the Milwaukee Bucks over the shutdown and health of Giannis Antetokounmpo late in the season and has determined that no further action is warranted, sources tell ESPN

item.description: The Pistons have downgraded Caris LeVert (right heel contusion) and Duncan Robinson (low back soreness) as questionable for Wednesday’s Game 5 against Cleveland. Kevin Huerter (left adductor strain) is also questionable.

item.title: Rome will again have a basketball team at the top …
item.description: Rome will again have a basketball team at the top European level, and it’s not Virtus Roma, the club that stopped existing in 2021. As “Gazzetta dello Sport” reports, on Tuesday afternoon (12/5), the Italian basketball federation is expected to formally approve the move of Vanoli Cremona Basketball, a club founded in 1999, to the Italian Capital.

item.title: Former Dallas GM Donnie Nelson is leading the …
item.description: Former Dallas GM Donnie Nelson is leading the consortium behind this move. Luka Doncic is among the investors, while former player Rimantas Kaukenas, who competed in Italy for Siena and Reggio Emilia, is also among the club representatives. What’s interesting in the report is that the club is also presented as interested in a wildcard for the EuroLeague or the EuroCup.

item.title: According to “L’Equipe“, football clubs are sceptical …
item.description: However, there’s no indication that the football club of the Italian capital and one of the biggest brands in European sports, AC Rome, is involved in all these plans. On the contrary, according to “L’Equipe“, football clubs are sceptical about the NBA European project. Echoing the older reports, the newest article on the matter adds to the football-related doubts. “From the start, the tension has been about the gap between the NBA’s financial expectations and the reality of the clubs and what t

item.title: NBPA releases statement on the passing of Brandon Clarke
item.description: The NBPA: Statement from NBPA Executive Director Andre Iguodala and NBPA President Fred VanVleet on the tragic passing of Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke.

item.title: I've been saying for over a week that I believe Steve …
item.description: Jake Madison: While I don't have a specific date or timeline my understanding is we are close to the end of the Pelicans head coaching search. I've been saying for over a week that I believe Steve Hetzel is the choice and still think that. Jamahl Mosley also remains a possibility

item.title: "You're talking about the greatest coach pretty much …
item.description: For a team involved in a hotly contested second-round series with the Minnesota Timberwolves, the Spurs are grateful they have the five-time NBA champion coaching legend available to them as a valuable resource. That's especially true for playoff newcomers like Johnson, Bryant and Vassell. "You're talking about the greatest coach pretty much of all time to be able to sit here and tell you the experiences he's been through or what he's seen," Vassell said. "That's second to none."

item.title: "He helps out a lot," Vassell said. "The film session, …
item.description: Popovich, 77, retired from coaching in May 2025 after suffering a stroke five games into the 2024-25 season. The NBA's all-time winningest coach remains the team's president of basketball operations. Vassell indicated the assistance Popovich has provided the team during the Western Conference semifinals hasn't been limited to just individual conversations with players. "He helps out a lot," Vassell said. "The film session, he helped out a lot. He just has so much wisdom, and that's just stuff yo

item.title: "I can't give away all coach Pop's secrets," Vassell …
item.description: Vassell declined to go into detail about the "feedback" the team has received from the Hall of Famer. "I can't give away all coach Pop's secrets," Vassell said. "He gives us a lot, but just being more physical, being ready, playing off two feet. I'll just say that for now." Vassell said how much advice Popovich offers players individually "depends on the situation." "He has a relationship with everybody and everybody's relationship is a little bit different," the sixth-year pro said. "He might t

item.title: “I got a little bit emotional,” World Peace admitted. …
item.description: I spoke with Metta World Peace again earlier today, and he reflected on seeing Wembanyama escape the hammer that fell so hard on him fourteen years ago. His reaction was a mix of veteran wisdom, empathy for a young star, and a touch of lingering “what-if” regarding his own career. “I got a little bit emotional,” World Peace admitted. “Sometimes you gotta win on the court and focus on the game. I wish I could not be able to be suspended and focus on the game. But, they ejected him and gave him a

item.title: Adam Silver on Jason Collins: His impact and influence extended far beyond basketball
item.description: Ian Begley: NBA commissioner Adam Silver issues statement on passing of Jason Collins: "Jason Collins' impact and influence extended far beyond basketball as he helped make the NBA, WNBA and larger sports community more inclusive and welcoming for future generations.”

item.title: Jason Collins told ESPN in November that he'd been …
item.description: Jason Collins told ESPN in November that he'd been diagnosed with stage 4 glioblastoma, one of the deadliest forms of brain cancer. He traveled to Singapore this past winter to receive experimental treatments not yet authorized in the United States. Those treatments were effective enough for him to return home, attend NBA All-Star Weekend events in Los Angeles and a game at his alma mater, Stanford. But the cancer returned recently, and Collins died peacefully at his home, surrounded by his fami

item.title: Collins was able to complete the initial parts of his …
item.description: Collins chose a treatment plan that he felt would give him the best quality of life, while also giving him a chance to extend his life beyond the initial prognosis. Collins was able to complete the initial parts of his treatment plan but the cancer returned too quickly for him to complete all of it. He is survived by his husband, Brunson Green, parents Portia and Paul Collins, and brother, Jarron Collins, who was most recently an assistant coach with the New Orleans Pelicans.

item.title: Indianapolis Colts general manager Chris Ballard and …
item.description: Indianapolis Colts general manager Chris Ballard and two-time NBA All-Star Michael Finley are part of the nine-person search committee to help select Wisconsin's next athletic director. Wisconsin announced the committee members Monday. Incoming Interim Chancellor Eric Wilcots is leading the search process because Jennifer Mnookin's tenure as chancellor will end Saturday ahead of her taking over as Columbia University's president on July 1.

item.title: Dave Rennie maintains he hasn’t yet decided who will …
item.description: Dave Rennie maintains he hasn’t yet decided who will captain the All Blacks, but he’s provided an insight into the process to pick his man. Back in New Zealand while his Japanese team on a bye, the new All Blacks coach revealed Kiwi NBA player Steven Adams had even been involved in the process. Adams, who plays for the Houston Rockets, was invited to a two-hour meeting with All Blacks greats Richie McCaw, Dan Carter and Keven Mealamu, and came armed with answers to questions sent by mental skill

item.title: Victor Wembanyama takes revenge as Spurs go up 3-2 over Minnesota
item.description: Victor Wembanyama set the tone early and never looked back as he led the San Antonio Spurs to a dominant 126-97 victory over the Minnesota Timberwolves in Game 5 on Tuesday. Wembanyama tallied 27 points, 17 boards, and five assists to put the Spurs one win away from their first West finals in nine years.

item.title: Timberwolves get rolled in Game 5. They've lost last …
item.description: Anthony Slater: Timberwolves get rolled in Game 5. They've lost last two in San Antonio by 67 combined points. Shot below 40% tonight. Julius Randle is now 26-of-71 FG (36%) in series. Anthony Edwards (41, 40, 39 minutes the last three) can use the two-day break. G6 Friday in Minnesota.

item.title: The San Antonio Spurs were a ratings drag during the …
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs were a ratings drag during the days of Tim Duncan, but the Victor Wembanyama era promises to be the opposite. Sunday’s Spurs-Timberwolves second round NBA playoff Game 4 averaged a combined 7.9 million viewers across Nielsen and Adobe Analytics, trailing only Sixers-Celtics Game 7 on NBC following the Kentucky Derby (10.99M) as the most-watched game of the ongoing NBA Playoffs.

item.title: Game 3 of the series Friday night averaged 5.25 …
item.description: Game 3 of the series Friday night averaged 5.25 million on Prime Video, marking the streamer’s largest NBA audience thus far (as of Friday). Viewership increased 24% from Thunder-Nuggets on ESPN last year (4.24M), and also notably surpassed Lakers-Thunder on Prime Video the previous night (5.08M). Game 2, as previously noted, delivered 5.2 million on ESPN last Wednesday — the most-watched game of the playoffs on the cable network. (Note that ESPN had aired only two second round games through Tue

item.title: Prior to the playoffs, the most-watched game of the …
item.description: While some of the credit may belong to Anthony Edwards and the Timberwolves — who have played in highly-viewed games in each of their past two playoff runs — the Spurs account for several high water marks this season. Prior to the playoffs, the most-watched game of the season was Spurs-Thunder on ABC/ESPN Christmas Day and the most-watched outside of broadcast television was the Knicks-Spurs NBA Cup Final on Prime.

item.title: Spoke with Brandon Clarke's mentor and former coach …
item.description: Drew Hill: Spoke with Brandon Clarke's mentor and former coach Tony Darden. The AZ basketball community is crushed. Tony shared these videos of his son AJ, who Brandon treated like a little brother. The first from draft night in 2019. "It was really hard to break the news"

item.title: Lakers unlikely to run it back with current roster?
item.description: According to Lakers and league sources, running it back with a mostly similar roster isn’t a palatable option. LeBron James is an unrestricted free agent. Austin Reaves will be one when he declines his player option. Both have played their way into big contracts, and retaining both could keep the Lakers from fully evolving into a roster built around Dončić. The promises made to Dončić and his representatives before he signed an extension with the Lakers weren’t to have one good month. And th

item.title: There is a desire for better center play, true lob …
item.description: There is a desire for better center play, true lob threats who mimic the skills of Daniel Gafford and Dereck Lively II (with cleaner injury histories). There is a desire for true two-way defensive players, wings with athleticism and energy on the defensive end and a sweet shooting stroke on the offensive side of the ball. And there’s a desire to lock in a long-term secondary playmaker next to Dončić. Reaves, who will decline his player option and become an unrestricted free agent after the sea

item.title: Rival executives predicted Reaves could command $40 …
item.description: While team and league sources believe that the Lakers and Reaves will eventually land on a contract that makes sense, the door for Reaves to walk is open should he get a big offer elsewhere. Rival executives predicted Reaves could command $40 million a season due to a combination of his play, his age and, perhaps most importantly, a free-agent class completely devoid of players as productive as him in their prime. At present, the Chicago Bulls and Brooklyn Nets, who both have needs at the guard

item.title: 76ers owner Josh Harris on Daryl Morey dismissal: We have failed to deliver in the way this city deserves
item.description: Sixers Managing Partner Josh Harris: “After speaking with Daryl, we determined that it was time for a fresh start. Bob Myers will lead the process of identifying a new leader and I believe his experience in constructing four NBA championship teams will be a valuable resource to our organization. To our fans, your frustration and disappointment are understandable and warranted. We have fallen well short of our own expectations and failed to deliver in the way this city deserves. That bothers me d

item.title: Bob Myers added: “I know how much the 76ers mean to …
item.description: Bob Myers added: “I know how much the 76ers mean to the City of Philadelphia, and it’s important we find the right leader to shape the future of this team,” Myers said. “The process will start immediately, and we will be thorough and deliberate in our evaluations. I believe this is a destination for top talent across the league and look forward to solidifying our infrastructure moving forward.”

item.title: Spurs had a moment of silence for Brandon Clarke and …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Spurs had a moment of silence for Brandon Clarke and Jason Collins. Keldon Johnson came into the NBA at the same time as Clarke. "He seemed like an amazing person and I always heard great things about him," Keldon told me. "It sucks. Sending condolences to his family."

item.title: Brooklyn Nets: We are heartbroken to learn of the …
item.description: Brooklyn Nets: We are heartbroken to learn of the passing of Jason Collins. Jason spent eight seasons in a Nets uniform, helping define an era of our franchise and playing a vital role on our back-to-back Eastern Conference championship teams in 2002 and 2003. He was a constant in our locker room - selfless, tough, and deeply respected by teammates, coaches, and staff alike. Those who were around Jason every day knew him not just as a competitor, but as a genuinely kind, thoughtful person who br

item.title: Jason Kidd: This one hurts. Jason Collins was a …
item.description: Jason Kidd: This one hurts. Jason Collins was a pioneer. He had courage like you've never seen. He was an incredible teammate. And having him in Brooklyn at the start of my coaching journey meant so much. Those who knew him were blessed to call him a friend. You are already missed my brother. Rest in power. 💔

item.title: Nick Nurse will remain as 76ers head coach
item.description: Shams Charania: Head coach Nick Nurse will continue in his role into a fourth season with the 76ers, sources tell ESPN. Four-time NBA championship architect Bob Myers, the president of sports for Harris Blitzer Sports & Entertainment, will be tasked to lead the search for the new head executive for the 76ers and he will oversee the franchise's basketball department in the interim.

item.title: Daryl Morey out as 76ers president of basketball operations
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: Daryl Morey is out as president of basketball operations with the Philadelphia 76ers after six seasons, sources tell ESPN. Morey and 76ers owners Josh Harris and David Blitzer met on Tuesday evening in Philadelphia and the decision was made to part ways.

item.title: Bryson Graham made the first changes to the Bulls …
item.description: Bryson Graham made the first changes to the Bulls front office Tuesday, according to multiple sources, by releasing several staffers, including Josh Malone, who as Windy City Bulls general manager oversaw the franchise’s G League affiliate.

item.title: Graham is expected to continue tweaking the front …
item.description: Graham is expected to continue tweaking the front office in the coming weeks, a process that could include hiring additional scouts and other support staff. The Bulls have not signaled whether they plan to continue with the structure of a general manager (previously Marc Eversley) directly under the executive VP with a support staff of assistant GMs.

item.title: While assembling his ideal staff, Graham also must …
item.description: While assembling his ideal staff, Graham also must appoint a new head coach. He will begin the initial stages of the coaching search this week, which includes requesting permission from teams to interview potential candidates. Graham previously suggested he was open to hiring an up-and-coming coach rather than seeking a veteran with championship experience, meaning the Bulls could look at current assistants on prominent coaching staffs.

item.title: Jake Fischer on LeBron James' offseason: The prevailing sentiment is he'd be best suited on a minimum deal
item.description: NBA insider Jake Fischer revealed the latest on the 41-year-old superstar, as James and the Los Angeles Lakers are about to face an uncertain offseason after being swept by the OKC Thunder in the second round of the NBA Playoffs. "There is definitely a prevailing sentiment around the league that LeBron, wherever he goes, would be best suited to take a minimum salary contract if he does care ultimately about winning another championship," Fischer said on Bleacher Report.

item.title: Joel Embiid for Domantas Sabonis swap mentioned as longshot scenario by rival teams
item.description: One longshot scenario that does get mentioned by rival team strategists is an Embiid-for-Domantas Sabonis swap with Sacramento … but even longshot might be underselling it. Both former All-Star big men could probably use a fresh start, but there's just no getting around the fact that Embiid, at 32, seems incapable of holding up for a deep playoff run. Which certainly shortens the list of potential suitors.

item.title: Dubai Basketball is finalizing agreements with the …
item.description: Dubai Basketball is set to add two standout EuroLeague players to its roster for next season. Dubai Basketball is finalizing agreements with the EuroLeague Best Defender Alpha Diallo and breakout rookie Mamadi Diakite.

item.title: “It was 2 games ago. It's the playoffs…I was focused …
item.description: ‪Tom Orsborn‬: Asked Wemby if he had any regrets about the elbow after having time to reflect: “It was 2 games ago. It's the playoffs…I was focused on the game today and now I'm focusing on the game in Minnesota in 3 days. It's the playoff. We got to move on and I got to care about my team.”

item.title: "I feel like the rage-baiting would have been maybe …
item.description: The production inside marked San Antonio's second-most points in the paint in a postseason outing since 1998, when it scored 72 in its 2008 playoff opener, and the most by any team in a postseason contest against an opponent that featured Gobert, a four-time NBA Defensive Player of the Year. "I feel like the rage-baiting would have been maybe one of the strategies [used by the Timberwolves]," Wembanyama said. "I feel like I need to stay composed."

item.title: Devin Vassell said the team needed to see an "upset …
item.description: Spurs guard Devin Vassell said the team needed to see an "upset Vic" Tuesday night in Game 5 of the Western Conference semifinals, while San Antonio coach Mitch Johnson preferred to use the word "mature" in describing the approach Victor Wembanyama would need to use to bounce back from his first career ejection. Wembanyama, meanwhile, had his own thoughts. "I feel they ain't mutually exclusive," he said. "I'm looking for both."

item.title: Youngest player in NBA history with 25+ PTS
item.description: StatMuse: Youngest player in NBA history with 25+ PTS 15+ REB 5+ AST 3+ BLK in a playoff game.

item.title: "I feel like we got the Vic you've seen all year," …
item.description: "I feel like we got the Vic you've seen all year," Castle said. "I think his maturity level was off the charts. He played smart, didn't really foul much, took the shots that were there for him. When he's playing like that, playing aggressively, with everything he brings for us defensively, I feel like we're hard to beat. Just where his mindset has been for the last 48 hours, I know he wanted to finish Game 4. But not letting that get to him and coming out and playing like that was a big reason w

item.title: Edwards: "I can't really pinpoint, like, why we keep …
item.description: ‪Jon Krawczynski‬: Edwards: "I can't really pinpoint, like, why we keep making the game plan mistakes. We just got to be better on it. Yeah, it's just that simple."

item.title: Anthony Edwards: 'Some of the stuff Wemby was doing you don’t really have too much of an answer for it'
item.description: ‪Chris Hine‬: Anthony Edwards postgame. On the slow start: “Some of the stuff Wemby was doing you don’t really have too much of an answer for it.” Then on breakdowns later in the game: “Game plan mistakes that we keep making, and it’s too late in the series to make these mistakes.”

item.title: Don’t see anyone in our locker room that’s too …
item.description: MrBuckBuck: Anthony Edwards: “I don’t see anyone in our locker room that’s too worried.” “Everything starts with Jaden McDaniels, trying to keep him out of foul trouble.” “Some of the stuff Wemby was doing you don’t have an answer for.”

item.title: “I feel like anybody who has any type of success, as …
item.description: “Who in the league do you think takes the most disrespect to their legacy right now?” DeMar DeRozan: “I feel like anybody who has any type of success, as soon as you fail and don’t win a championship or something, it’s so easy to discredit players these days because everybody is so opinionated. I remember growing up, the only time you heard something about a player was when you picked up the paper the next day. You know what I mean? Nowadays, you got people on Twitter thinking they’re journalist

item.title: DeMar DeRozan on Gregg Popovich: He tricked me into becoming a playmaker and it opened up the game for me
item.description: DeMar DeRozan: I think I didn’t realize it when I got to San Antonio. My mind was always like, ‘I got to shoot it thirty, thirty-five times. I’m getting after it.’ You know what I mean? That was always my mindset. When I got with Pop, Pop taught me how to use my teammates to make it easier for me later in the game. Utilizing them, playing a certain type of way, breaking down the game, understanding the game, sharing the ball — that opened up the game for me super easy. You know what I mean? And

item.description: The NBA's much-discussed new “3-2-1” lottery format is expected to win approval in a May 28 Board of Governors vote to be implemented next season, which would present teams with the fourth-through-10th-worst records in the league with an equal 8.1% shot at winning the No. 1 overall pick. Those impeding changes have led various NBA executives here to tell me that they think we're going to see fewer future picks traded for star players in the near term … at least until front offices get a better f

item.title: The other thing is that he has made it clear to the …
item.description: Shams Charania: But, yes, the big question is medical: what happened with the cramping? He’s telling teams, like he told Ramona Shelburne last week, that it was due to an excess of creatine intake. That’s how he has addressed it with teams. Moving forward, he believes he’s passed this issue. Washington and Utah will bring him in. They’ll have conversations. They’ll see his medicals and be able to review them. Two other things he has gotten across during this process: he wants to be the number on

item.title: That aura, that respect, and what he commanded from us …
item.description: Dwyane Wade: Our situation was a little different because Stan was a young coach, a very good young coach. I thank Stan so much for my early success. But we’re talking about the Godfather. We’re talking about one of the greatest coaches of all time coming back. That aura, that respect, and what he commanded from us was just totally different. We had a bunch of grown-man knuckleheads. We needed to be brought in. We needed to be pulled together. We weren’t the most talented team, but we had a lot

item.title: Sources say Morey had two expensive years left on his …
item.description: League sources say Morey had two expensive years left on his contract through the 2027-28 season thanks to an extension he signed in December 2023. Sixers ownership nonetheless decided it would stomach the cost of parting ways immediately.

item.title: One source with knowledge of the situation insisted …
item.description: One source with knowledge of the situation insisted Tuesday that the widely criticized trade of McCain to Oklahoma City for a 2026 first-round pick via Houston and three second-round picks was not the proverbial last straw. Ownership very much approved the McCain deal and sources say that Sixers leadership feels quite confident in its post-Morey scouting ability to ensure that a quality player is selected at No. 22 overall next month.

item.title: Andre Iguodala not a candidate for Sixers' job
item.description: I'm also told Myers will interview candidates internally as well as externally to take over … but also that it's far too early to name any possible favorites for the role. One item, however, that has been spelled out rather definitively: For all the recent rumblings about Myers' affinity for former Golden State Finals MVP Andre Iguodala, I’m told Philadelphia does not plan to consider the ex-Sixer and current executive director of the NBA Players Association as a potential candidate for its new

Sunday’s Spurs-Timberwolves second round NBA playoff Game 4 averaged a combined 7.9 million viewers across Nielsen and Adobe Analytics, trailing only Sixers-Celtics Game 7 on NBC following the Kentucky Derby (10.99M) as the most-watched game of the ongoing NBA Playoffs. The last non-clinching early round game to average as large an audience was Warriors-Lakers Game

item.title: “F--king people booing, how about that?” Green said on …
item.description: Warriors star Draymond Green had his opportunity and, when he stepped to the stage, was greeted with a familiar noise from the crowd: boos. “F--king people booing, how about that?” Green said on “The Draymond Green Show.” “You go to a stage as a comedian, not a comedian, but I'm doing a roast. And people are booing before you start. “The one thing comedians don't want is to be booed,” Green continued. “I'm getting booed before I started. Thankfully, I get booed in every arena I go into, so it wa

item.title: Equipment managers are every team's unsung heroes
item.description: Michael Redd: Saw it's the NBA equipment manager conference in Chicago. Let me tell you... Equipment managers are every team's unsung heroes. They help players in every way and, often, put them above their own families. Lot of respect for the guys who helped me with everything from washing my jersey and keeping my favorite sneakers at the ready, to making sure my wife found her seat OK and that my charity event went off without a hitch. The amount of work put in, vs the amount of financial rewar

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns, an avid card collector, revealed …
item.description: Karl-Anthony Towns, an avid card collector, revealed that he was outbid for a one-of-one Randy Orton WrestleMania 41 patch autograph. The winning bid came in at $42,100 on eBay. “This has us SICK,” Towns wrote on his Instagram account “bigbodegascards,” which is dedicated to his card collecting. “Thought it was ours for the taking, until a last-minute NUCLEAR bid snuck in! Who has this card now!? We have to know!”

item.title: No one could have fought this cancer with more grace …
item.description: Ramona Shelburne: No one could have fought this cancer with more grace and courage than my friend, Jason Collins. Devastated for him, his family and this world to lose such a bright light. But what a legacy he’s left us.

item.title: Mark Madsen: Jason was one of the strongest and …
item.description: Mark Madsen: Jason was one of the strongest and kindest peple I have ever meet. He was ferocious on the court and full of love and kindness off the court. He welcomed everyone no matter your background. He was the guy who brought fun and energy to life. This is a beautiful statement from Jason’s family and I too will miss Jason dearly. "Jason changed lives in unexpected ways and was an inspiration to all who knew him and to those who admired him from afar. We are grateful for the outpouring of l

item.title: NBC inexplicably faded to black then cut to commercial …
item.description: The Spurs announcer asks for a moment of silence for the recent passing of Brandon Clarke and Jason Collins. NBC inexplicably faded to black then cut to commercial in the middle of the moment; a DraftK*ngs commercial played on the broadcast.

item.title: He showed a lot of courage to live his life proudly. …
item.description: Brevin Knight: I said crazy day because my Stanford family also lost Jason Collins. He showed a lot of courage to live his life proudly. We supported him as much as he supported every teammate he had and person he came in contact with. Condolences to his family! 🙏🏾🙏🏾

item.title: “I’m walking with a GOAT right here,” Morant said in …
item.description: In another post, Morant shared a photo of himself with Clarke and Jaren Jackson Jr. Morant later shared a post from Grizzlies social media manager Devin Walker that featured him with Clarke during the 2019 NBA Summer League when he won MVP. “I’m walking with a GOAT right here,” Morant said in the video as the pair walked off the court.

item.title: I’m going to really miss BC!! 😢 Gone way too soon! He …
item.description: Brevin Knight: Damn I’m going to really miss BC!! 😢 Gone way too soon! He was a genuinely good dude and humble from the first day we met him!! Just a crazy day. One last time for you, Shout out Canada!! 🙏🏾🙏🏾

item.title: “Just a really good person, a really good teammate, …
item.description: On Tuesday afternoon, Minnesota Timberwolves forward Kyle Anderson was one of several former and current Memphis players who paid tribute to Clarke. “Just a really good person, a really good teammate, obviously someone who was really good on the court. Just someone I could instantly see and we would start laughing and start sharing jokes. He was Memphis’ own, for sure. He did his part in the community,” Anderson told The Athletic. “It’s just crazy. It really don’t make no sense. I was on the pho

item.title: “He was the most low-maintenance dude,” Few told The …
item.description: “He was the most low-maintenance dude,” Few told The Athletic. “He was just so easy to coach. Every time you’d see him walking around the facility, he had a smile on his face. Just a warm, kind spirit, and a heck of a player. Our team that year could’ve won the national championship. He was fun to be around.”

item.title: “He was an incredible player, but an even better …
item.description: Clarke had one year remaining on his contract with Memphis, where he had established himself as a beloved teammate. Niele Ivey, a former Grizzlies assistant coach who is now the head coach of Notre Dame’s women’s basketball team, told The Athletic that she was “heartbroken” to hear of Clarke’s death. “He was an incredible player, but an even better person,” she said. “I have nothing but great memories of his positive energy, enthusiasm and the way he uplifted everyone around him — teammates and

item.title: Celtics not expected to make major push for Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Jake Fischer on Giannis Antetokounmpo: ‘The Celtics have come out and said that they love Jaylen Brown and that they feel good about him. I do know that Boston made a call to the Bucks before February's trade deadline, but I do not think Boston ever loomed as a very serious suitor, and until I hear otherwise, I don't think they're necessarily going to be either.’

item.title: Agent: Lakers can't 'just offer LeBron James a number' to keep him
item.description: A source familiar with LeBron James' thinking told ESPN that the Lakers' approach in how they show their interest in retaining him will be a factor, beyond the simple dollar amount. "The Lakers can't just offer James a number. They have to offer a why behind that number," an agent who does not rep James but has had clients on L.A.'s roster during James' tenure, told ESPN. "Their plan for any discount would have to be coherent."

item.title: James marched off into the L.A. night, simmering from …
item.description: And so -- without even stopping to change clothes -- James marched off into the L.A. night, simmering from another perceived indignation delivered by the organization that, as one source close to him told ESPN, tried to "push him out the door," after acquiring Doncic last season.

item.title: Team sources told ESPN that it was clear the injury …
item.description: He missed all of training camp, the preseason and the first 14 games of the regular season, later admitting that the injury prompted to "question yourself at times if you feel like you're going to be able to get back to what you know you can do, and do at this level." Team sources told ESPN that it was clear the injury weighed on James and his demeanor. Even so, whatever rancor that James' side might have felt, a source familiar with the Lakers' thinking told ESPN their relationship with James w

item.title: Despite the risk of losing this year’s pick, Pritchard …
item.description: At 29, Ivica Zubac should be in his prime. The Pacers likely needed to pay a premium for his deal, which will pay him just $42.1 million over the next two seasons. Additionally, the rest of the lottery is likely to be dotted with point guards. With Haliburton, the Pacers are set there, so protecting a pick further into the lottery had diminishing value for Indiana. Despite the risk of losing this year’s pick, Pritchard said the trade framework allowed the Pacers to keep most of their future draf

item.title: “If he played for any other team in the league,” …
item.description: Dylan Harper just turned 20 in March. On Tuesday, he crossed the 250-minute plateau in his 10-game-old playoff career. And he’s already comfortable getting buckets on the big stage. Lookin’ just like his daddy. “If he played for any other team in the league,” fellow Spurs rookie Carter Bryant said, “he’d be starting and probably be winning the Rookie of the Year right now. And to see how he’s sacrificed and bought into his role, it’s amazing.” San Antonio has outscored opponents by 73 points wit

item.title: I don’t think Luka Doncic is a point guard. I don’t …
item.description: Brandon “Scoop B” Robinson: When people see a player of Luka’s size and vision, the comparisons immediately fly toward LeBron James and Magic Johnson. Do you see him in that same lineage of “Point Gods,” or is he something else entirely? Rashad Phillips: I don’t think that he’s like none of them, but I do think that he plays the same position as LeBron James. Magic Johnson was a point guard. I don’t think Luka Doncic is a point guard. I don’t think LeBron is a point guard. I think they’re Dual F

item.title: As soon as the lottery was revealed Sunday, the …
item.description: As soon as the lottery was revealed Sunday, the immediate question around the league and industry was whether the Utah Jazz, holding the No. 2 pick, might make an eventual overture to trade up to No. 1 to select AJ Dybantsa. This is factoring in the strong ties between Utah owner Ryan Smith, CEO Danny Ainge and president of basketball operations Austin Ainge to BYU.

item.title: While the connection there is logical, there's no …
item.description: While the connection there is logical, there's no indication that it will happen at this stage of the predraft process, with lottery teams having only recently been assigned their selections. At the moment, the thought of Utah trading up is purely speculative, as the Jazz are sitting in a strong position at No. 2 regardless: They can simply wait and see who Washington takes, something that is not being treated as a foregone conclusion.

item.title: Dybantsa has shaped up as the favorite at No. 1, but …
item.description: Dybantsa has shaped up as the favorite at No. 1, but that's not the consensus, with most around the NBA having seen Peterson as a strong candidate for that spot all season, and varying opinions on Boozer's and even Caleb Wilson's candidacies. The talent gap between that foursome, particularly Dybantsa and Peterson, might not ultimately be wide enough to trade up from No. 2 to 1 in a vacuum, regardless of which teams owned which picks.

item.title: Tracy McGrady to join Wagner basketball program as strategic advisor
item.description: Shams Charania: Naismith Hall of Famer Tracy McGrady has taken a role with Wagner College as strategic advisor for the men's basketball program, joining his son Laymen who committed to the team, per ESPN sources. McGrady will lead Wagner's basketball operations, recruiting and name, image and likeness efforts, and joins a number of current and former NBA players to take administrative positions in college, such as John Wall (Howard), Stephen Curry (Davidson), Shaquille O’Neal (Sacramento State),

item.title: LeBron James views JJ Redick hire as ‘one thing the Lakers got right’
item.description: LeBron James has a "great" relationship with JJ Redick, sources close to him say -- with one telling ESPN that Redick's hiring is "one thing the Lakers got right." But James, who played the past eight seasons in Los Angeles and helped deliver the franchise its 17th championship in 2020, saw Pelinka's priority in that moment as yet another example of the Lakers taking him for granted, sources said. Adding to James' ire, sources said, was the fact that the past dozen or so wins in that stretch cam

item.description: But we are uncovering contrarian opinions over the course of the last 48 hours at the NBA Draft Combine, when the front offices of all 30 NBA teams, players, agents, and media are essentially clustered in the same gym and hotel for the entirety of the day. First, as it relates to Washington, I asked a high-ranking scout on one of the other lottery teams if they considered Washington's selection of Dybantsa a certainty and if that would impact their own preparation. item.title: While there's more public speculation about other …
item.description: So what will the Jazz do at No. 2, especially considering Cameron Boozer's dad, Carlos Boozer, currently works for the organization in a scouting role? Most expect Kansas guard Darryn Peterson to be the Jazz's pick here. While there's more public speculation about other options, sources around the combine speak with almost the same level of confidence that Peterson goes No. 2 as they do Washington taking Dybantsa at No. 1.

item.title: All told, the Lakers made 1.3 excess playoff …
item.description: Was that a success? Well, it resulted in negative Finals probability added in the end, but not by a huge amount — because there wasn’t much to begin with — and it did add to the tally of playoff appearances above expected for the Lakers during LeBron James’ time with the club. And generally, that was the story for his L.A. tenure aside from 2020: the Lakers exceeded playoff-odds expectations more often than not, and when they fell short in the playoffs, it didn’t cost them too much. item.description: Through 2025-26, LeBron has played eight seasons with the Lakers. Within our sample going back to 1983-84, we actually have three other distinct eight-season eras of Lakers basketball: the Showtime era (1984-1991), the Kobe/Shaq era (1997-2004) and the Kobe/Pau era (2007-2014), reflecting the years both stars played together — or in Magic’s case, the years leading up to his initial retirement in 1991 due to contracting HIV. item.description: Seattle and Las Vegas are already getting teams, so there’s nowhere else to go. First of all, the league is only exploring expansion. I assume it will come to fruition, but there are plenty of good reasons to pump the brakes, and some owners are going to remain unconvinced that it’s smart to further divide the pie with two more teams. item.title: And as has been the case in MLB, many involved in …
item.description: Jump shooting is the single most foundational skill in basketball, and it’s been a largely subjective art for its entire existence. A dozen shooting coaches might give you 10 different nuanced approaches to shooting an ideal jumper. Now, though, the game’s most important repetitive motion is on the verge of being deconstructed by tech that has finally caught up. The concept draws regular comparisons to baseball, which has undergone its own biomechanics revolution across the last decade-plus—the

item.description: At least theoretically, if you understand these general jumper traits, you can determine an “optimal” combination of shot angle and shot speed for a given player and shot type. “Pitch design is really popular in baseball,” said Buffi, who previously worked for the Los Angeles Dodgers. Reboot Motion also began in the baseball space, expanding to the NBA in the last few years. “We’re trying to pioneer shot design in basketball.” As Buffi and others in the field are quick to point out, though, the

item.title: Josh Giddey undergoes successful right ankle arthroscopy
item.description: Chicago Bulls: Medical Update: Josh Giddey recently underwent a successful right ankle arthroscopy. Giddey is scheduled to start basketball activities in approximately three months and is expected to return for the start of training camp.

item.description: ALL NBA Podcast: The Wolves are getting just 0.85 points per direct touch from Julius Randle these playoffs. That's the second-worst mark in the Tracking Era for a player with 300+ direct touches in a single playoff run.

In other words, 94 percent of the picks in June’s draft are likely not to make your dreams come true, either because: * They’re not good enough, injured, under-nurtured, or similar; * Or by the time they’re highly productive, they’ve moved on to another team. item.title: But is there any reason to believe a center picked …
item.description: Is a 25th pick a useful tool for the Lakers to upgrade over former top overall pick DeAndre Ayton? Ayton just had an awful season, for him, with an EPM of −0.58, producing 3.3 Earned Wins. Over his seven-year career, he has averaged about five Earned Wins a season. He’s at an age where he should be peaking, but instead he’s had two of his worst years. Who knows what he’ll be like next year. "It's fun to me," he says. "The way I see it, the fans, the people that watched the games and root against us, they want their team to win. You will never hear an Oklahoma City Thunder fan complaining about my free throws. You will never hear a Lakers fan complaining about LeBron or Luka's free throws." H

item.title: "There's a standard everybody here conforms to," …
item.description: The Thunder have anywhere from eight to 12 players who could be starters on other teams, so why are so many of them content to sublimate their egos for the betterment of this one? "There's a standard everybody here conforms to," All-Star center Chet Holmgren says, "but I don't think anybody who is brought in here has to make changes to themselves or how they go about things. Everyone has innate principles to their lives that we all share."

item.title: Terry Rozier appeared in front of an arbitrator last …
item.description: The NBA and former Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier appeared in front of an arbitrator last month to again argue whether he should have been paid this season, three sources briefed on the hearing said. It is the second time the league and the player have asked an arbitrator to decide whether Rozier should receive his $26.6 million salary as he faces federal charges over his alleged involvement in a sport gambling scheme.

item.title: Scripps Sports has scored local rights to its first …
item.description: Scripps Sports has scored local rights to its first NBA team, as the Pistons will air games over-the-air with the company beginning with the 2026-27 season. Terms of the deal were not disclosed. Games will air in the Detroit DMA, surrounding areas and across the state of Michigan. The move to Scripps comes after the team had long been on FanDuel Sports Network Detroit, which folded following the season. This will mark the first time local Pistons games will primarily be on free TV since 2005.

item.title: Sources told SBJ that the Hornets were among the teams …
item.description: Sources told SBJ that the Hornets were among the teams considering a deal with streaming platform Victory+. Sources also told SBJ last month that Fubo makes surprise pitch to all 13 NBA teams that fled Main Street. DAZN, Gray TV and Nexstar have also been reported as local options for teams. The Pistons’ former RSN also was the home to the Tigers and Red Wings, but those clubs – both owned by the Ilitch family -- launched Detroit SportsNet in collaboration with MLB Media.
