item.title: A Houston jeweler whose clients include NBA and NFL …
item.description: A Houston jeweler whose clients include NBA and NFL celebrities has been indicted on federal charges over allegations he used his business to launder and export gold acquired by a network of scammers targeting older people. Zohaib Muhammad and three other people were arrested May 12 on charges of conspiracy to commit wire fraud and conspiracy to commit money laundering.

item.title: The Sharpstown jeweler has made headlines in recent …
item.description: Muhammad and another of the indicted men, Samir Ali, own ZoFrost and Co. Custom Jewelry. The Sharpstown jeweler has made headlines in recent years by making diamond-encrusted jewelry for famous athletes, including NBA MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander. and NFL Pro Bowler Maxx Crosby. The store also designed Simone Biles’ engagement ring and collaborated in the design of the Oklahoma City Thunder's NBA championship rings.

item.title: First-round pick candidate Christian Anderson remains in the draft
item.description: Texas Tech star guard Christian Anderson, a third-team All-American and projected top-20 pick, is keeping his name in the NBA draft, his agent Aaron Mintz of CAA Basketball told ESPN. Anderson was all-in on the draft process when he announced his initial decision in early April, and recent positive feedback from the NBA draft combine and pre-draft workouts for NBA teams reaffirmed his choice to go pro.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama trading card sells for $5.11 million
item.description: Victor Wembanyama's 1-of-1 numbered 2023-24 Panini Prizm Black parallel has sold for $5.11 million in a private sale via Fanatics Collect, Fanatics confirmed to ESPN. It's by far the most ever spent on a Wembanyama card, and the fourth and 11th most spent on a basketball and a sports card, respectively. It's also the most expensive known sale of a non-autographed NBA card and the third sports card sold for $5 million in 2026.

item.title: Mark Daigneault on Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: 'I think he gives the team extreme confidence'
item.description: Brandon Rahbar: Mark Daigneault on SGA: "I think he gives the team extreme confidence. I think his consistency of approach and tone has a lot to do with our ability to stay steady even if things are unsteady."

item.title: Mitch Johnson to reporters in OKC on the Spurs' …
item.description: Jared Weiss: Mitch Johnson to reporters in OKC on the Spurs' offense tonight: "In this type of game, you gotta be sure of everything you’re doing in a secure, mature way."

item.title: "It just felt like it was a little bit of everything, …
item.description: Tom Petrini: "It just felt like it was a little bit of everything, in terms of we did not put ourselves in position enough to be successful in each possession... to beat a team of this caliber in their building with mistakes we'll need to be a lot better to give yourself a chance." Mitch

item.title: The Thunder have not lost back-to-back playoff games …
item.description: Law Murray: The Thunder have not lost back-to-back playoff games the last two postseasons. Have nine straight wins following a loss in the playoffs.

item.title: Thunder outscored Spurs 26-15 on second chance points …
item.description: Law Murray: Thunder outscored Spurs 26-15 on second chance points in Game 5 of the 2026 Western Conference Finals That's the most second chance points the Thunder have scored in any playoff game under Mark Daigneault

item.title: The insider said, “I don’t think it’ll be long before …
item.description: Windhorst added in a follow-up that Cleveland may explore acquiring Milwaukee Bucks superstar Giannis Antetokounmpo. The insider said, “I don’t think it’ll be long before you start to hear the Cavs attached to Giannis Antetokounmpo. If they put Evan Mobley on the table, what if they can turn him into Giannis?”

item.title: “I speak from experience,” Hammon said Tuesday. “Allen …
item.description: Those comments have generated some buzz after Brunson received the Larry Bird Trophy for MVP of the Eastern Conference finals after the Knicks swept the Cleveland Cavaliers on Monday night. He averaged 25.5 points and 7.8 assists in the series. The Knicks will face defending champion Oklahoma City or San Antonio in the finals. “I speak from experience,” Hammon said Tuesday. “Allen Iverson got MVP and he lost in the finals. I think the two best teams are probably in the West, but I’m up for being

item.title: This quote introduces the first issue, according to a …
item.description: In the ESPN interview, Peterson said creatine supplements were the cause of the cramping, including an extreme, full-body cramping episode that ended up with Peterson in an ambulance and ultimately at the hospital. “I’d never taken it before (going to college),” Peterson told ESPN. “But after the season I took two weeks off and they did tests which showed my baseline level was already high. So, they said when I dosed (a process of increasing a dose over time to create maximum benefit at the begi

item.title: “There isn’t a blood test that I know of that measures …
item.description: Dr. Brian Schulz, a sports medicine specialist and orthopedic surgeon at Cedars-Sinai Orthopaedics in Los Angeles, who is also the team physician for the Los Angeles Angels and the Anaheim Ducks, is completely familiar with the use of creatine and how athletes use it as a supplement, but was at a loss when read the above quote and asked about a test that could measure creatine levels. “Yeah, I’ve never heard of that,” Schulz said in an interview with the Deseret News. And he isn’t alone. Dr. Dav

item.title: A number of doctors said that after hearing this …
item.description: A number of doctors said that after hearing this story, including Peterson saying he feared for his life after the day that he ended up in the hospital, it sounded more like a case of PTSD after a scary event (possibly just severe dehydration, without any underlying condition), than it sounded like a case of recurring cramping. “I thought I was going to die on the training table that day,” Peterson told ESPN, later adding that he was worried it would happen again. “Whenever I felt anything like

item.title: Thunder take 3-2 series lead with big win over Spurs
item.description: Justin Martinez: Final: Thunder 127, Spurs 114 Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: 32 points and 9 assists Alex Caruso: 22 points and 6 assists Chet Holmgren: 16 points and 11 boards OKC claims a 3-2 series lead on a night when it didn't have Jalen Williams and Ajay Mitchell. It can book its ticket to the NBA Finals on Thursday.

item.title: Mitch Johnson waves the white flag. Thunder are gonna …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Mitch Johnson waves the white flag. Thunder are gonna take Game 5 and a 3-2 series lead. Massive game from the reserves, basically the inverse of last game all around. Chet’s best game of the series. Caruso and McCain were huge. Probably Wemby’s least impactful game of the series

item.title: Jared McCain tonight
item.description: Brandon Rahbar: Jared McCain tonight 20 PTS 3-8 3PT 3 REB “Sold high.”

item.title: That's on pace to be the biggest jump in 3P% from the …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Alex Caruso is shooting 47.7% from three this postseason, 18.4% higher than he did in the regular season (29.3%). That's on pace to be the biggest jump in 3P% from the regular season to the playoffs in NBA history (min. 50 attempts in both).

item.title: Looked totally overmatched physically by Isaiah …
item.description: Kevin O'Connor: Victor Wembanyama: 4/15 with 6 boards. Looked totally overmatched physically by Isaiah Hartenstein. Jaylin Williams also did a great job on him. This can't happen in a must-win Game 5. Straight-up needs to be better for the Spurs to obviously have any chance.

item.title: They’re far enough below the $209.1 million first …
item.description: The Clippers are entering the 2026 offseason with a very fluid salary cap situation. They have 14 players under contract totaling $171.8 million in salary, including the 5th overall pick in the 2026 Draft. They’re far enough below the $209.1 million first apron to use several roster-building mechanisms to improve the team. That will likely remain the threshold they operate around, since they don’t mind paying the luxury tax.

item.title: They can now generate up to $30 million, but doing so …
item.description: That plan probably changed at the trade deadline. Their acquisition of Darius Garland, while giving them a better player than one they could realistically have signed in free agency, also wiped out a meaningful chunk of their cap space. They can now generate up to $30 million, but doing so would also require renouncing the cap holds for all of their free agents. Not only would they lose several players on team options to access that room, but they would also be sacrificing the Bird rights to Ben

item.title: President Trump is considering making an appearance at …
item.description: President Trump is considering making an appearance at the N.B.A. finals next week, a rarity for an American leader, after his hometown New York Knicks clinched a championship spot, according to three people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander on Alex Caruso: 'He's one of if not the best competitor in the NBA '
item.description: Shai on Alex Caruso: "He has the most championships on this team. He's played in the most big games on this team. He's one of if not the best competitor in the NBA night in and night out. He wears that hat for this group all season long and it's on TV every night now so the world's getting to see it but he's huge for us"

item.title: "I'm definitely not on Twitter. Especially not this …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Shai: "I'm definitely not on Twitter. Especially not this time of year" bro hasn't tweeted in 2 years lol

item.title: "19 of em? Atta boy."
item.description: Brandon Rahbar: Chet Holmgren was asked about Jared McCain. "How many did he shoot?" He was told it was 19 shots. "19 of em? Atta boy."

item.title: “They send so many bodies towards him. It's hard at …
item.description: ‪Tom Orsborn‬: Castle on how difficult it is to get Wemby going in the face of OKC’s defense: “They send so many bodies towards him. It's hard at times…It’s tough, but yeah, I mean, he's our best player, so yeah, we need him to be aggressive.”

item.title: “It just felt like it was a little bit of everything …
item.description: ‪Tom Orsborn‬: Mitch on Spurs defensive collapse: “It just felt like it was a little bit of everything in terms of we did not put ourselves in position enough to be successful in each possession... to beat a team of this caliber in their building…we'll need to be a lot better to give yourself a chance."

item.title: This organization is built and is still building the …
item.description: Jordi Fernández: "Yeah, it's been growth, and I've had to learn how to be in this position. Obviously, when you get the job, you always believe that you're ready for it, and even though you've coached and you've been a head coach before, probably basketball is the easiest part of it. But then you have the head coaching duties and what it means, and the amount of people that you're responsible for, and the amount of people that you've got to touch every day. I think that's been a great journey s

item.title: "I've learned that from especially Mike Brown, Michael …
item.description: Jordi Fernández: "I've learned that from especially Mike Brown, Michael Malone, and Sergio Scariolo is when somebody's investing in you, you almost work not just hard, but harder. Because you want to give back. It's the feeling that somebody believes in you and that you're responsible to do a job. It makes you have that willingness to do it right, do it for the team in the right reasons, because you want to grow and you want to take those steps, whatever your goals are in your career. But I thi

item.title: Kenny Smith: 'For my first six years I was always jealous of Scottie Pippen'
item.description: Kenny Smith: "I don't think I've ever said this, for my first six years I was always jealous of Scottie Pippen. I was so jealous because I was the sixth pick, he was the fifth. I visited Chicago. They brought me in on an interview. I was so jealous because I was like, I knew what he could do and bring out of you. You always have it in you, but you sometimes you need somebody to elevate it to another level. And I knew that he would do that. So I used to look at Scottie and be so jealous of that.

item.title: Kenny Smith: 'We created what the podcast world is now'
item.description: Kenny Smith: "I'm going to say it, and this is not offensive, but we created what the podcast world is now. Because the way we behaved allowed people to have more of a freedom of speech. And that's what I always loved about Chuck. We were really good before Chuck came and Chuck just threw gasoline on the fire. Chuck has allowed people to see a different side of me because now I get asked questions that I never got asked before."

item.title: "Bill Russell was my first coach. Bill Russell, he had …
item.description: Kenny Smith: "Bill Russell was my first coach. Bill Russell, he had a rule, I had to sit next to him on every plane ride and every bus ride as a rookie. He would talk and he'd say stuff like, 'Yeah, Kenny, back in our day, I was with, me and Malcolm X would go over here and we go eat.' I'm like, 'Malcolm X?' He's like, 'Yeah, Malcolm X.' He would like, 'Yeah, Martin Luther King Jr.' He would tell me stories about them like they were so casual. The people who made me, like him."

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: 'I don’t ever worry about Chet. I’ll bet on Chet 10 times out of 10'
item.description: Brandon Rahbar: SGA on Chet Holmgren: “He was really good. Chet has all the tools and everybody in our building and the league knows that. He’s a hell of a player. I don’t ever worry about Chet. I’ll bet on Chet 10 times out of 10.”

item.title: "If it was 4 or 5 me's out there, we would've been …
item.description: Brandon Rahbar: SGA spending a lot of his media availability praising his teammates. He says tonight was one of the worst starts he's ever had. "If it was 4 or 5 me's out there, we would've been down 20 after the 1st quarter."

item.title: SGA on what stands out about his OKC teammates: "How …
item.description: Brandon Rahbar: SGA on what stands out about his OKC teammates: "How hard they work. The intentions they have out there on the basketball court and the kind of teammates they are. Nobody cares about anything besides winning."

item.title: "The way they guard, how physical they are we don't …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Stephon Castle on the Thunder: "The way they guard, how physical they are we don't get that same luxury to be able to play as physical on the other end at times"

item.title: Wembanyama scored 20 points, his lowest of the series, …
item.description: Now down 3-2 in the series, they'll try to extend the matchup — and save their season — in Game 6 at San Antonio on Thursday night. Wembanyama scored 20 points, his lowest of the series, and only a 12-for-12 effort from the foul line helped him get there in Game 5. He was 4 for 15 from the floor, missing all five of his 3-point tries, never seeming to get into any sort of rhythm. He offered an impassioned speech to teammates during a timeout barely two minutes into the third quarter, after the T

item.title: think they wanted the game more than we did,” he added
item.description: ‪Tom Orsborn‬: Champagnie said OKC was the “more desperate team.” “I think they wanted the game more than we did,” he added.

item.title: Leon Rose puts his arm around his son, Sam, and …
item.description: Leon Rose puts his arm around his son, Sam, and visibly breaks down in tears after the emotions set in of the Knicks going to the NBA Finals. This is beautiful stuff. We love you, Leon. Thank you for everything you have done.

item.title: Bulls to interview Johnnie Bryant
item.description: Marc J. Spears‬: The Chicago Bulls have received permission to interview Cavaliers associate head coach Johnnie Bryant for their head coach opening, a source said. The former University of Utah guard has 12 years of NBA assistant coaching experience and has also been an associate head coach with the New York Knicks.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama declined to speak with media after Game 5
item.description: Sam Amick: Victor Wembanyama, who struggled mightily in the Spurs’ Game 5 loss in OKC, declined to do media this evening. He just left the PayCom Center as midnight central time nears.

item.title: "He's got to take more than 15 shots," Johnson said. …
item.description: A team spokesman said Wembanyama would be unavailable to speak to reporters, nearly an hour after Spurs coach Mitch Johnson said "we're going to need to" get San Antonio's generational superstar going to stave off elimination Thursday in Game 6 with the Thunder now leading the series 3-2. "He's got to take more than 15 shots," Johnson said. "Even with the [12] free throws, he's going to have to score more than 20 points for sure."

item.title: Victor Wemanyama had only 1 assist and 2 turnovers …
item.description: Iztok Franko: We've seen Harden's assists to turnover stats floating around throughout the playoffs. Victor Wemanyama had only 1 assist and 2 turnovers last night. He had as many (or more) turnovers as assists in 9 of 15 games in the playoffs and is averaging 2.8 turnovers and 2.8 assists.

item.title: Devin Vassell summed up the defensive collapse this …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Devin Vassell summed up the defensive collapse this way: “Undisciplined. Undisciplined rotations, undisciplined fouls. That's about it.”

item.title: Fox acknowledges he's a man who thrives off routine, …
item.description: As teammates wearing Nike tech fleece, jeans and hoodies scurry through the locker room to board SUVs and buses awaiting outside that will take the San Antonio Spurs to an optional team dinner after their Game 3 victory over the Minnesota Timberwolves in the conference semifinals, Fox stuffs both hands into his pockets and shakes his head. The 28-year-old "old man" of this young group has made up his mind. "It's already 12:30. I will not be at the dinner," Fox said. "We can do brunch tomorrow."

item.title: "It allows me to be more consistent, more …
item.description: Fox says he firmly believes the steadier his life is off the court, the more his game can grow. It's how he continues to rise every time he falls fighting through a high right ankle sprain that has hobbled him since Game 4 against the Wolves on May 10. "It allows me to be more consistent, more levelheaded," Fox told ESPN. "If I'm not making shots, I feel like I don't get sporadic. Even when I'm making shots, my game doesn't change. I think I stay kind of level, through it all."

item.title: Isaiah Hartenstein: "We go out there and try to be …
item.description: Brandon Rahbar: Isaiah Hartenstein: "We go out there and try to be physical. They're super physical too."

item.title: "I think he's a special human being. I think just in …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: IHart on Jared McCain: "I think he's a special human being. I think just in general, no one will change the way he is. I've never seen someone so happy every single day. I can't be mad looking at him"

item.title: “Shai is trying to sell shoes; I went on a stream with …
item.description: “The best team is the team that has an awareness of each person’s individual goals and helps them get there as a team,” he said earlier this year in a conversation with The Athletic. “Shai is trying to sell shoes; I went on a stream with his shoes. Maybe somebody’s trying to find their rhythm. They’re not really shooting the ball too well. It’s like, let me pass up this (shot), get them a good one. Stuff like that.

item.title: Chet Holmgren: 'Ego gets in the way of maximizing the moment and also understanding'
item.description: Instead, he would play within the Thunder’s team concepts. “Ego has been the downfall of many people’s careers,” Holmgren said. “I feel like ego gets in the way of maximizing the moment and also understanding. Basically, what you’re asking me is, would I trade what we just accomplished last year and the opportunity that we have (this year) and the group that we have? Would I sacrifice that to go be able to shoot 20 shots a game? “I don’t think so.”

item.title: “The way I see it, when you’re in it, you have to look …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Tracy McGrady asked Chet Holmgren if he views Wemby as a rival for years to come: “The way I see it, when you’re in it, you have to look at it as Thunder vs Spurs. We’re trying to win these games. They’re trying to win these games. That’s how you have to attack it”

item.title: Mason Plumlee after trucking Jared McCain in the back, …
item.description: Mason Plumlee after trucking Jared McCain in the back, "I got another one for you too."

item.title: Shane Blakeney has officially withdrawn from the 2026 …
item.description: Jon Rothstein: South Carolina's Shane Blakeney has officially withdrawn from the 2026 NBA Draft and will return to school next season, per his agents Kevin Martin and Oshwand Scott. Transfer from Drexel who averaged 14.2 PPG last season.

item.title: There was robust debate among stakeholders studying …
item.description: Giving the bottom three teams reduced lottery odds already takes a big step toward ensuring the worst teams actually finish with the worst records. On top of that, the NBA is now saying those teams could finish with the 10th, 11th and 12th picks in the draft if they fail to be selected in the lottery before those spots. There was robust debate among stakeholders studying the topic, sources said, about where to put the line for the floor for these teams. Some argued it should be closer to eighth,

item.title: While ESPN's Brian Windhorst wrote about the "secret" …
item.description: While ESPN's Brian Windhorst wrote about the "secret" room, sources expect the lottery itself -- not just airing of the results -- to become a live, televised event. No more conspiracy theories. All of it happening in real time. The potential for tremendous theater.

item.title: Joey and Jesse Buss eye investment in Tony Parker’s ASVEL Basket
item.description: Former Los Angeles Lakers owners Joey and Jesse Buss are interested in being among the investors in French EuroLeague team ASVEL. ASVEL has long been considered one of the teams that might leave the elite league, but they’ve changed their mind and will continue to play in it. Now, they are ready to take a step further and secure significant funds.

item.title: Shane Blakeney preliminarily entered the NBA Draft …
item.description: South Carolina basketball transfer portal addition Shane Blakeney preliminarily entered the NBA Draft earlier this offseason. However, as of Wednesday’s deadline, GamecockCentral’s Wes Mitchell has confirmed that the Palmetto State native has pulled out of the draft and will be a Gamecock for the 2026-27 season.

item.title: Argentine police arrested two Chilean citizens …
item.description: Argentine police arrested two Chilean citizens suspected of being part of a transnational gang that orchestrated burglaries at the homes of well-known athletes, including stars from the NBA and NFL.

item.title: Targets also included the homes of NBA players Luka …
item.description: According to local police, the two Chilean citizens were part of a crime ring that targeted the homes of prominent athletes in the U.S. and Argentina, including NFL stars Patrick Mahomes and Travis Kelce and former tennis player Juan Martín Del Potro. Targets also included the homes of NBA players Luka Doncic and Mike Conley Jr.

item.title: LeBron James waiting on Lakers
item.description: Speaking during Wednesday's episode of Brian Windhorst & The Hoop Collective (beginning at the 49:40 mark), Windhorst said he has been told that James is waiting for the Lakers to approach him with an offer and a plan. Windhorst noted that James and his camp want the Lakers to make a contract offer, and if it is for anything less than the maximum, they want the Lakers to explain why and what other players they are going to target with the money that is saved.

item.title: “The refs are cheating,” Ross wrote on social media. …
item.description: Throughout this year’s NBA playoffs, fans have ripped countless referees for apparently giving the Thunder preferential treatment. Well, it appears a former first-round pick is now entertaining that conversation. Terrence Ross, who played in the NBA from 2012-2023, accused the refs of “cheating” during the Western Conference Finals. “The refs are cheating,” Ross wrote on social media. “Point blank period.”

item.title: There are longtime assistants waiting for first shot …
item.description: K.C. Johnson: This is another example of wide net Bryson Graham is casting. There are longtime assistants waiting for first shot on list, candidates with previous head coaching experience and up-and-coming younger assistants. Process remains in early stages.

item.title: The OnlyFans model, who goes by BlueBearI, took to X, …
item.description: ‪After one of the women who is frequently seen behind San Antonio Spurs coach Mitch Johnson was identified as an OnlyFans (OF) model, their status has been revealed for Game 6 against the Oklahoma City Thunder. The OnlyFans model, who goes by BlueBearI, took to X, formerly Twitter, before the Spurs' Game 5 loss the OKC, to reveal her status along with the other woman, Julie J Swan, for the next contest. “Yes, we will be at Game 6,” Blue wrote.

item.title: Kendrick Perkins found himself in the middle of the …
item.description: Ex-NBA player Kendrick Perkins found himself in the middle of the action at his son's AAU game over the weekend ... when he lunged at the opposition in a heated altercation -- but his side says he was simply defending his players.

item.title: Donald Trump to attend NBA Finals game?
item.description: President Trump is considering making an appearance at the N.B.A. finals next week, a rarity for an American leader, after his hometown New York Knicks clinched a championship spot, according to three people familiar with the matter. An appearance by Mr. Trump at Madison Square Garden would be the latest incidence of a high-profile sports event serving as a backdrop for his presidency. Mr. Trump last year showed up at the Super Bowl, the Daytona 500, the U.S. Open men’s final and a Yankees game.

item.title: If the Knicks had lost Game 4 of the Eastern …
item.description: If the Knicks had lost Game 4 of the Eastern Conference finals on Monday, Mr. Trump had planned to make a surprise appearance among the team’s faithful at Madison Square Garden on Wednesday, according to three people familiar with the planning. But after the Knicks completed a sweep of the Cleveland Cavaliers on Monday, the president is turning to the N.B.A. championship.

item.title: “I really like Jim Dolan a lot, I’m really happy for …
item.description: Mr. Trump has said in recent weeks that he remains a Knicks fan and has been following the team’s progress. “I really like Jim Dolan a lot, I’m really happy for him and the team,” Mr. Trump said of James Dolan, the owner of the Knicks, the New York Rangers and Madison Square Garden, on WABC-AM, known as TalkRadio 77. “I think it’s great.”

item.title: “It’s funny, because it’s Chet,” said Kenrich …
item.description: The Thunder center had not been sleeping. He wasn’t stationed in a back seat away from the action. He wasn’t hanging with the coaching staff, who sit in a different section, sequestered from his teammates’ social activity. Yet, he stood up to blurt out a major announcement, one he was certain no one on the plane had yet realized. “Oh!” he yelled. “Jamal Murray had 53! Y’all see that?!” Without hesitation, the Thunder players merged, all cackling simultaneously. Holmgren realized what had happene

item.title: He’s the highly intellectual individual who juxtaposes …
item.description: He’s the comedic relief inside an unusually tight locker room that was built organically, either through the draft or with players who were shunned by other organizations and came to Oklahoma City when they were young, only for the franchise to develop them into helpful contributors. He’s the anchor of the NBA’s most smothering defense, the deserving second-place finisher in Defensive Player of the Year voting in 2026. He’s the highly intellectual individual who juxtaposes long diatribes about b

item.title: NBA issues warning to Victor Wembanyama after skipping media obligations
item.description: Jeff McDonald: The NBA has officially warned Victor Wembanyama about violating media access rules, per a league source. Wembanyama declined to address the media after the Spurs' Game 5 loss last night in OKC.

item.title: Sources say star-level players at high-major schools …
item.description: Sources say star-level players at high-major schools can easily gross between $4-to-6 million per season. The result: Several players and agents have to calculate what they might be giving up and how those figures compare with projected draft slots according to the NBA's rookie scale. Players are no longer merely seeking first-round assurances. They want to know the likelihood that they'll be able to secure a maiden NBA deal that matches, if not surpasses, their would-be salaries for staying in

item.title: One such strategist told me this week that, if tasked …
item.description: I've heard some strategists suggest that teams in Utah's position might seek as many picks as possible between 2027 and 2029 because the lottery will be just too tantalizing for the next three years. One such strategist told me this week that, if tasked with finding a new trade home for Giannis Antetokounmpo, he would advise the Bucks to seek as many picks in the same draft year as possible if they are higher on, say, 2028's projected class of draftees compared to 2027 or 2029. That way Milwauke

item.title: #Hornets pre-draft workout group today includes Nick …
item.description: Rod Boone: #Hornets pre-draft workout group today includes Nick Boyd (Wisconsin), Wyatt Fricks (Marshall), Nate Johnson (Kansas State) and Tre White (Kansas), per league sources.

item.title: Against Phoenix in the first round, Shai …
item.description: Against Phoenix in the first round, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander fell 11 times on his shot attempts. In the Lakers series, that same number bumped up to 13 falls. With 22 falls in this series, his fall frequency has increased significantly, and he has now fallen twice as often in the Western Conference finals on a per-game basis than he did in the first-round sweep over the Suns.

item.title: On those seven falls in the last two games, …
item.description: On those seven falls in the last two games, Gilgeous-Alexander drew whistles on four of them, raising his foul rate on falls to an even 50% in the playoffs (23 out of 46). That is, when he falls on shot attempts this postseason, he is getting a foul call, on average, every other tumble to the ground.

item.title: Hawks promote Onsi Saleh to President of Basketball Operations
item.description: Shams Charania: The Atlanta Hawks are signing general manager Onsi Saleh to a long-term contract extension and promoting him to President of Basketball Operations, sources tell ESPN. Saleh – runner up for 2026 league executive of the year – took over as Hawks GM last offseason after joining Atlanta in 2024 following executive stints in Golden State and San Antonio.

item.title: Baba Miller was sick and could not participate in the …
item.description: James Ham: Baba Miller was sick and could not participate in the workout today in Sacramento.

item.title: Following Olympiacos' EuroLeague triumph on Sunday, …
item.description: Following Olympiacos' EuroLeague triumph on Sunday, Nikos Tsakos welcomed the trophy to his company's offices during a shipping conference. There, he also made a surprising remark about the possibility of a friendly game between Olympiacos and the Knicks. "We support the team of our hometown and the city where we grew up. I was born in Piraeus and moved to New York at 17," he said, via Sport24. "We are happy that the two teams are among the most historic in the world. Soon we will be able to do

item.title: His son, Panagiotis Tsakos, went even further when …
item.description: His son, Panagiotis Tsakos, went even further when discussing the idea. "It's something we are discussing with the team," he said regarding a potential exhibition game. "Either for the inauguration of the renewed stadium, or at Madison Square Garden. As you know, with half a million Greeks, we could fill the venue."

item.title: The NBA’s rationale for not fining Victor Wembanyama, …
item.description: Sam Amick: The NBA’s rationale for not fining Victor Wembanyama, and instead issuing him a warning, took into account that he’s considered a first-time offender on this front. That part is standard procedure, though I did think they might come down harder on him because of the magnitude of the moment (it’s…the West Finals).

item.title: Donald Trump said he plans on attending an NBA Finals …
item.description: Stefan Bondy: Donald Trump said he plans on attending an NBA Finals game after being invited by James Dolan

item.title: Donte DiVincenzo to return during the 2026-27 season?
item.description: It is far too early in the recovery process to predict when Donte DiVincenzo will return to action. Tearing an Achilles tendon is among the most serious injuries in the game, often keeping players out for a year or more. Boston’s Jayson Tatum returned in less than 10 months, but that is considered an outlier. Still, DiVincenzo is trying to follow Tatum’s lead. He flew to New York for surgery less than 24 hours after suffering the injury, following a similar timeline that Tatum went through. DiVi

item.title: Several within the Timberwolves organization believe …
item.description: Several within the Timberwolves organization believe DiVincenzo could return at some point next season. The question is how much can he be expected to give? Tatum played remarkably well after returning this season, but he suffered a couple of injuries to his calf and knee in the playoffs and missed Boston’s Game 7 loss to Philadelphia.

item.title: “Just knowing that you’re being thought of, you’re …
item.description: In two short years, he has stitched himself into the fabric of this team and this city. And he can’t wait to be back. “Just knowing that you’re being thought of, you’re loved, it helps you, it gives you the motivation to want to get back,” DiVincenzo said. “There’s nothing more that motivates me than trying to get in front of and play in front of our fans.”

Barnes made sure to include books from Wembanyama’s favorite genre, science fiction, and more specifically a favorite author, Sanderson. item.title: Though Wembanyama has not participated in the …

It shows that someone can be one of the best basketball players in the world and also love reading. They want the world to know that reading is cool. Wemby is cool. Science fiction and fantasy and world-building and magic are cool.

item.title: NJ Transit
item.description: NJ Transit won’t change World Cup plans to give Knicks fans a direct ride to a historic playoff home game — favoring soccer tourists over local sports lovers, officials said. New York City’s on-fire hometown team will host the NBA finals at Madison Square Garden for the first time in nearly 30 years — potentially including a high-stakes Game 6 on June 16, the same day a match between France and Senegal is set to kick off at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, NJ.

item.title: Transit riders traveling to Madison Square Garden for …
item.description: NJ Transit riders traveling to Madison Square Garden for the Knicks game at 8:30 p.m. June 16, against the Thunder or Spurs, will need to switch to PATH trains to get into Manhattan at the 33rd Street station, a spokesperson for NJ Transit said. Asked if the policy could be tweaked due to the potential World Cup-NBA Finals crossover, a spokesperson said no changes will be allowed.

item.title: Out of the 100 players who were top-five picks from …
item.description: Out of the 100 players who were top-five picks from 2000 to 2019, 44 won a playoff series as a rotation player for their original team. That leaves 56 who never fulfilled their team's heavy draft investment with even a single playoff advancement.

item.title: On average, top-five picks from 2000 through 2019 …
item.description: The dream of a decade-plus of production for a franchise cornerstone also doesn't materialize very often. On average, top-five picks from 2000 through 2019 lasted 5.1 seasons with their original team, with 56% lasting until their fifth year (and their second contract) and 44% leaving before then. Only 6% played at least 10 seasons with their initial team. (Tatum will bump that figure to 7% next season.) And for now, only five of the 100 top-five picks in that period are still with the team that

item.title: They are donating hundreds of free tickets to …
item.description: The Knicks are looking out for their fans. They are donating hundreds of free tickets to underprivileged youth for their home games in the Finals, The Post has learned. Underserved New York families affiliated with their Garden of Dreams Foundation will receive 250 tickets per home game. That means a guaranteed 500 tickets across Games 3 and 4. If there is a Game 6, that number will rise to 750.

item.title: Many claim they had been ruined by that ancient sports …
item.description: Fans nonetheless gathered outside the arena on Monday night after the Knicks completed a sweep of the Cleveland Cavaliers. The police said that conditions were less raucous than they had been at the Knicks’ most recent home game on Thursday, but there were six arrests, and fans scaled subway entrances, drank in public and blocked traffic. Longtime die-hards have rued the cancellation of the official watch parties. Many claim they had been ruined by that ancient sports enemy: fake fans — posers —

item.title: “Everybody just wants to get a viral clip out instead …
item.description: Social media influencers have become bandwagon Knicks fans, capitalizing on how the formula has been shown to draw audiences. That, in turn, has led to grousing online. One Reddit user said last week that it felt like “half of the people” outside the Garden were just there to mug for the cameras. “Everybody just wants to get a viral clip out instead of actually celebrating the win,” the user wrote, adding, “It just feels fake.”

There’s just one problem: the Knicks didn’t win a championship in 1993. Their last NBA title came in 1973.

Not yet. “When I took the job, I thought about it,” Brown said during a Zoom conference call with reporters on Sunday. “But going through this process, one of the things that I’m trying re

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns: Mike Brown had big shoes to fill because Thibs did an amazing job
item.description: “He had big shoes to fill, because Thibs did an amazing job and gave us the experience and the education and the opportunity to show the world what we could do as a team,” All-Star center Karl-Anthony Towns said after Game 3 against Cleveland. “When Mike came in, making the Eastern Conference finals was going to be the bar — minimum.”

item.title: Draft lottery expected to become live televised event
item.description: Pro: A live lottery! While ESPN's Brian Windhorst wrote about the "secret" room, sources expect the lottery itself -- not just airing of the results -- to become a live, televised event. No more conspiracy theories. All of it happening in real time. The potential for tremendous theater. How a new lottery would play out is yet to be determined. The NBA's current method -- the first ping-pong ball selected determines the first pick, and so on -- is certainly one possibility, although that would el

item.title: So there's still a lot to get through with Giannis. …
item.description: Dru: “New York was a big key player [for Giannis], & so we'll see how they finish out this season & they can still be in the mix as well. So there's still a lot to get through with Giannis. And I do think maybe I'll talk about, tomorrow maybe, a mystery team out there.” @ChrisBHaynes

item.title: Victor Wembanyama also needs to be a dominant …
item.description: Victor Wembanyama also needs to be a dominant defensive force, considering Oklahoma City connected on 49% of its shots with him on the court in Game 5, which ranks as the best field goal percentage a team has achieved so far this postseason against the 22-year old, according to ESPN Research. Widely proclaimed the best player currently in the NBA, Wembanyama needs to show it for San Antonio to stave off elimination. And though he has shocked observers with a seemingly otherworldly skill set, Wem

item.title: “I’m honored that — something I’m going to tell my …
item.description: Just as Curry was an evolution of him, Reggie Miller sees Wembanyama as an evolution of the bigs that came before. Miller now hopes that, as part of the NBC revival in his second career as a broadcaster, the players who leave their mark will remember that he was a part of the journey, too. “I’m honored that — something I’m going to tell my kids — I called Victor Wembanayama’s first playoff game,” Miller says. “What’s going to be cool is some of my best moments as a basketball player were on NBC.

item.title: "Victor Wembanyama, one of the rare few capable of …
item.description: Ricky Rubio: "Victor Wembanyama, one of the rare few capable of changing the NBA game, like Stephen Curry, Michael Jordan, or Shaquille O'Neal."

item.title: Keyon Dooling on his involvement in NBA healthcare scheme: 'It was greed'
item.description: Keyon Dooling: I have a a steep hill to climb on my road to redemption. It wasn't justifiable. It was greed. I don't blame anybody who introduced me to it or anything like that. I assume my responsibility in the whole act.

item.title: Tyrese Maxey is officially a girl dad. On Wednesday, …
item.description: Tyrese Maxey is officially a girl dad. On Wednesday, Myra Gordon announced on Instagram that her and the Philadelphia 76ers guard's daughter, Marvel, had been born May 14. Gordon had announced back in November that the two were expecting a child. Gordon also posted a photo of Maxey and herself with Marvel on her Instagram story. Marvel is Maxey and Gordon's first child together. Gordon played college basketball at the University of Alabama and Pepperdine.

item.title: NBA approves new anti-tanking rules: Draft lottery expands to 16 teams
item.description: Shams Charania: Breaking: The NBA's Board of Governors has passed new anti-tanking rules that include expanding the draft lottery from 14 to 16 teams, a relegation zone where the bottom 3 teams get penalized with lessened chances for the No. 1 pick, and flattened odds, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: The NBA's Board of Governors vote passed 29-1 -- with …
item.description: Shams Charania: The NBA's Board of Governors vote passed 29-1 -- with the Memphis Grizzlies as the lone vote against the new anti-tanking lottery reform, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Hometown power couple Stephen and Ayesha Curry and …
item.description: Hometown power couple Stephen and Ayesha Curry and Michelin-starred restaurateur Michael Mina are teaming up to bring three new luxury dining options to uptown Charlotte late this year. Multi-phase renovations are under way at The Ritz-Carlton, Charlotte. By winter, the premium hotel will debut three experience-led concepts, according to a news release and interviews given exclusively to CharlotteFive ahead of Thursday’s announcement:

item.title: The collaboration marks a homecoming for the Currys — …
item.description: The collaboration marks a homecoming for the Currys — who met while growing up in Charlotte and visit regularly — and solidifies their ties with the The MINA Group, which operates the original location of The Eighth Rule in San Francisco and pairs with Sweet July at the Regent Santa Monica Beach. “The partnerships that we’re entering into with Michael Mina and Ayesha and Steph Curry are not just exciting for The Ritz-Carlton, Charlotte — I think they are super exciting for Charlotte as a whole,”

item.title: Thomas Sorber is optimistic he will be ready for Summer League
item.description: Sorber has progressed throughout the season and is now able to take part in team workouts. He’s optimistic that he’ll be able to play this year in the NBA Summer League and feels like he’s made the most of this unexpected rookie apprenticeship. “I’m still reading the scouting report. I’m still seeing who’s a hot shooter, who to worry about, who not to worry about,” he said. “Just trying to get the game plan in my head so when I am ready next year to play, then I’ll be able to, you know, already

item.title: “The first couple weeks I was hurt, (I was) definitely …
item.description: Sorber said the organization’s experience with situations like his helped steady him mentally as he works to get back on the court. “The first couple weeks I was hurt, (I was) definitely devastated. But each and every week, the guys instilled confidence into me, telling me that I’m going to get through this, you know?” he said. “Stick to what the trainers have to tell you, and just work hard. “And then, with my work ethic, it’s like I know I’m gonna be back the same player — probably even better

item.title: "Just worry about what we have to do to be better. Not …
item.description: Jared Schwartz: Mikal Bridges, having been to a Finals earlier in his career, on the advice he can share with his Knicks teammates: "Just worry about what we have to do to be better. Not get too into the media and all the stuff. A lot of questions, a lot of talk about how great we are, how great we’ve been. That doesn’t matter, we just gotta worry about being ourselves and stay locked in. And go win."

item.title: The league will also wield expanded disciplinary …
item.description: The league will also wield expanded disciplinary authority to address tanking, including the ability to reduce teams' lottery odds, modify teams' draft positions and impose significant fines on offending teams as part of the new lottery format.

item.title: Under this proposal, all 16 teams would be in the …
item.description: The Nos. 9 and 10 play-in seeds in each conference receive two lottery balls each, and the losers of the 7-8 play-in games receive one lottery ball each. Previously, the league drew odds for only teams with the bottom four records in the league, while the other 10 lottery teams were ordered by inverse record. Under this proposal, all 16 teams would be in the drawing -- one that, sources said, likely will be done live next year, either going from 1 to 16 or, like it is unveiled currently, from 16

item.title: Fertitta Entertainment, the Houston company that owns …
item.description: Texas billionaire Tilman Fertitta has long pursued a merger of his sprawling casino and restaurant business with Nevada’s iconic Caesars Entertainment. He’s finally pulling it off. Fertitta Entertainment, the Houston company that owns Las Vegas’ Golden Nugget, restaurant giant Landry’s and chains like Rainforest Cafe and Morton’s, is acquiring Caesars in a $17.6 billion deal that combines two of the nation’s largest casino, hotel and restaurant empires.

item.title: Grizzlies opposed lottery reform over rule barring teams from three consecutive top-5 picks
item.description: Shams Charania: Sources said the Grizzlies voted against the new lottery reform solely because of the new rule disallowing a team from picking top 5 in 3 consecutive years. As @BobbyMarks42 says, the Grizzlies have the more favorable 2027 first-rounder of Cleveland, Minnesota and Utah, and because the Jazz again have a top-5 pick this year, their first in 2027 cannot be in the top-5 under new rules.

item.title: NBA still has to finalize some final mechanisms, …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The NBA still has to finalize some final mechanisms, sources say, regarding how the new draft lottery will truly unfold, most importantly what would happen if a team does draw a top-five pick for a third year in a row, or the No. 1 pick in consecutive years.

item.title: One general manager told me: "I'm not trading any …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The uncertainty surrounding the NBA's new draft lottery format, plus it's three-year probationary period, will spark different reactions from front offices. One general manager told me: "I'm not trading any picks beyond 2029." More @TheSteinLine here: http://tinyurl.com/45m57axf

item.title: As I currently understand it, if the Jazz's 2027 …
item.description: Jake Fischer: As I currently understand it, if the Jazz's 2027 first-rounder that's owed to Memphis, and can't be top-five next year, were to land at No. 5, it would be slated at No. 6. But, there's also the possibility that the league would just discard that outcome and draw again for No. 5.

item.title: The NBA announced today that 38 players have notified …
item.description: NBA Communications: The NBA announced today that 38 players have notified the league that they wish to be removed from the list of “early entry” players eligible for selection in the NBA Draft 2026. Following the NBA’s early entry withdrawal deadline of 5 p.m. ET on June 13, 2026, the NBA will announce the list of any additional players who provide the NBA with timely notice that they wish to be removed from the list of “early entry” players eligible for selection in the NBA Draft 2026.

item.title: A defendant in the federal NBA gambling case told a …
item.description: A defendant in the federal NBA gambling case told a judge today that he paid an NBA player to change his performance so he could bet on him. The player, a federal prosecutor later clarified, was former Charlotte Hornets and Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier. Through his attorney, Rozier disputed the claim. The defendant, Marves Fairley, made the admission Thursday as he pleaded guilty to seven felony counts in two separate cases brought by the Department of Justice over the last year. Federal prosec

item.title: "There are some desperate men in this case with …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: A lawyer for Rozier denies that Rozier was paid to change his performance. "There are some desperate men in this case with terrible criminal records and tons of exposure, and they know what to say to please these prosecutors," Jim Trusty, Rozier's lawyer, said.

item.title: AJ Dybantsa on potentially being the No. 1 pick: 'It's very important...I don't plan on dropping in the draft'
item.description: NBA Courtside: AJ Dybantsa says being the number 1 pick is very important to him: “It’s very important. I been number 1 since my first rankings came out. ESPN ranked me number 1 in 9th grade. I was like 14 and I ain’t drop. So I don’t plan on dropping in the draft” (Via @allthesmokeprod )

item.title: Victor Wembanyama becomes fifth player to accumulate 350 points, 150 rebounds in first playoff appearance
item.description: Michael C. Wright: Victor Wembanyama now ranks as the fifth player to produce 350 points & 150 rebounds in his first career postseason appearance, per ESPN Research.

item.description: Tim MacMahon: Jalen Williams is active, but OKC’s starting lineup remains the same as Game 5.

item.title: According to NBA president of league operations Byron …
item.description: According to NBA president of league operations Byron Spruell, Grizzlies general manager Zach Kleiman and team president Jason Wexler were both heavily involved in the process. Kleiman played an active role in the league’s general manager committee discussions, while Wexler participated in Board of Governors meetings, where Memphis ultimately became the lone organization to vote against the proposal.

item.title: "If you were to grandfather in already traded picks, …
item.description: While the reform is designed to further discourage tanking and flatten lottery odds across the league, Memphis appears to be the franchise most directly affected by one of its unintended consequences. Evan Wasch, NBA executive vice president, head of basketball strategy and growth, explained how the restrictions with the Utah pick travels to the acquiring team in the new system. The league didn't want to create an imbalance in value on traded and non-traded picks. "If you were to grandfather in

item.title: When he can be aggressive, it's very impactful."
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Mitch Johnson said Dylan Harper’s injury, OKC’s defense have combined to slow him down in recent games. “For the most part I give a lot of acknowledgement to what OKC is doing, and Dylan's had some really good moments as well. ... When he can be aggressive, it's very impactful."

item.title: That’s not the plan. We’d like to be clean, but we'd …
item.description: Clemente Almanza: Mark Daigneault on OKC’s offense: “I thought what our approach did in Game 5 was it gave us a margin for error to be a little bit sloppy, and we were. That’s not the plan. We’d like to be clean, but we'd much rather approach the game like that.”

item.title: "They got all the marks of a great defense. They've …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Thunder coach Mark Daigneault on what makes the Spurs defense so tough: "They got all the marks of a great defense. They've got, obviously, the rim protection, speaks for itself, but they have great point of attack defenders that are highly aggressive and that really make you uncomfortable on the perimeter with that behind them. And then they're competent, they've got schemes, they're organized, they're disciplined, they're well coached, so all the all the things you need to be a re

item.title: Mitchell Robinson suffered a broken pinky; no timetable for return
item.description: Knicks center Mitchell Robinson broke his right pinky finger earlier this week, and there is no timetable for his return, league sources told The Athletic.

item.title: “We've seen these guys now, these this team 10 times …
item.description: Tom Petrini: “We've seen these guys now, these this team 10 times this year, and we've seen them the last five games that we've played, and we won a couple, and they beat us three times, and so there's no Lombardi speech coming, or magic line. We're going to go out there and try to play our standard of basketball with the proper approach, and try to execute through all the things that we're going to be seeing at that moment.” -Mitch Johnson

item.title: “The takeaway from any of the games that we've won or …
item.description: Clemente Almanza: Mitch Johnson on the officiating talk: “The takeaway from any of the games that we've won or lost has not been that the refs decided it.”

item.title: “Sometimes players make shots or miss shots, …
item.description: Clemente Almanza: Mitch Johnson on how they’ve limited SGA: “Sometimes players make shots or miss shots, especially players of his caliber. You can get at times intoxicated of what you think may be working or not working. I think we've learned that throughout this playoffs.”

item.title: Stephon Castle on Thunder: 'We feel like collectively that we're better than this team'
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Stephon Castle: "We feel like collectively that we're better than this team"

item.title: I don't think scoring against them has been a problem …
item.description: Tom Petrini: "I just think all of our focus and attention was on the defensive end. I don't think scoring against them has been a problem for us. Our self-inflicted mistakes, like turnovers and allowing them to get offense rebounds and easy buckets, is what slows us down. So, I think when we're focused on defense and we're getting stops and being able to get out and run and get easy looks, it makes the game pretty simple for us." -Steph Castle

item.title: "I think a lot of my turnovers were unforced. I know …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Steph Castle on limiting his turnovers: "I think a lot of my turnovers were unforced. I know that they like to pressure a lot, but I don't think a lot of my turnovers came from their pressure. I think it was kind of self-inflicted. Getting in there, taking my time, and playing off two feet is what's been working for me."

item.title: For him to come in and give us a breather and …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Steph Castle on Carter Bryant: "For him to be a rookie and come in and there's no dropoff defensively. That alone says a lot. For him to come in and give us a breather and defensive rebound for us and try and make minimal mistakes is big because we need those minutes. In the playoffs, every possession matters. So, when he's doing that and making it tough on (SGA) and tiring him out, in the long run, it definitely helps."

item.title: Carter has been as coachable as anybody. At times, he …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Asked Mitch Johnson about Carter Bryant's night and his willingness to be coached hard. "There's much to do with what happened a couple games ago. I know, in the visual of it... Carter has been as coachable as anybody. At times, he probably is trying to do the right thing too much, and I think that's where, at times, the urgency and the competitiveness of myself comes out because I just want that kid to play fast and aggressive. He has good raw instincts, and when he plays free and

item.title: "First thing is listening to the experienced people, …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Wemby on his first-ever Game 7: "First thing is listening to the experienced people, whether it's on our team, on our staff, or outside, we got the chance to have plenty of those around."

item.title: "We did what we needed to do, trusted the game, …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Wemby: "We did what we needed to do, trusted the game, trusted the basketball Gods"

item.title: He’s obviously not 100%. He didn’t know what to …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Mark Daigneault on Jalen Williams: "He’s been hellbent on trying to get himself to this point. I give him credit. He’s a big team guy, big competitor. He’s obviously not 100%. He didn’t know what to expect, I didn’t know what to expect. So it was a matter of getting him out there in an insulated role and see what he could bring to the team. … He hasn’t done a full return to play the way that he would.”

item.title: There will be at least FIVE Game 7s this postseason, …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: There will be at least FIVE Game 7s this postseason, tied for the most in a single postseason in NBA playoff history. It's the most in one season since 2016.

item.description: Law Murray: This will be the first conference finals Game 7 since 2023 Heat at Boston, and the first WCF Game 7 since 2018 Warriors at Houston. The road team has won the last four conference finals Game 7s. Oklahoma City Thunder have now lost all three Game 6s under Mark Daigneault.

item.description: The Nuggets have never been big spenders, which is why the belief around the league is that Cameron Johnson (on an expiring $23 million deal) or Christian Braun (on a five-year, $125 million extension signed in the fall) is likely to be moved to create enough room to give Watson something in the per-year range of those players. One point of potential concern is Watson's recurring hamstring injuries, which cost him a large chunk of the second half. Unfortunately, the injury came right after the b

item.title: Walker Kessler likely to re-sign with Utah
item.description: But given that neither Jackson nor fellow big man Lauri Markkanen is a full-time center, Kessler remains an important player in Utah's path back to contention in the Western Conference. The consensus is that Kessler is signing a new deal with the Jazz in the range of $25 million to $30 million a year. "It appears Utah is gearing up to keep him," an East scout said. "I don't see an obvious fit in free agency for someone to try to get him. They're going to be in an interesting spot next season, an

item.title: Kyrie Irving to Lakers not likely to happen?
item.description: As far as the Los Angeles Lakers' chances of pulling a potential deal around Irving, Fischer dismissed speculations that the franchise will try to reunite the star point guard with Luka Doncic in the wake of Austin Reaves' free-agency situation. "My only real understanding of what the Lakers want to do from a timeline perspective is they want to bring in a talent that fits 27-year-old Luka Doncic's timeline. As great as that pairing of Luka and Kyrie was, I think Kyrie is a little bit redundant

"If he wants to get a max, they'll tell him to go get one," an East executive told ESPN. "But he's Cade's guy, so they'll have to play it the right way.

item.title: The belief overall is that Grimes will land something …
item.description: The belief overall is that Grimes will land something at or below the midlevel exception, and ultimately, it will come from the 76ers. "I do think he stays there out of pressure," a West executive said. "You have to keep at least one of [Grimes or Oubre], if not both, after that trade. "And if they don't go into the tax, they're going to get crushed."

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: Spurs were the aggressors tonight from start to finish
item.description: Nick Gallo: Shai postgame: "They were the aggressors tonight from start to finish. They played harder than us, hit more shots, were more aggressive, were in attack mode. We were on our heels. We kept it in a manageable spot, but the second half he just blew it out of proportion."

item.title: There was the long robe that the San Antonio star wore …
item.description: There was the long robe that the San Antonio star wore to his home arena on Thursday night, done to celebrate an Islamic holiday but also reminding some of his look last summer at a Shaolin temple he visited while seeking physical, mental and spiritual growth. There was the freshly cropped hair, another sign that he was all business. “I’d seen a picture pregame,” Spurs guard Devin Vassell told NBA TV afterward. “I knew he was locked in from there, for sure.”

item.title: The robe that he wore to Thursday’s game, he confirmed …
item.description: The robe that he wore to Thursday’s game, he confirmed in French during his postgame news conference, wasn’t an homage to his time last June in China at the Shaolin temple — but rather to celebrate Eid al-Adha, an Islamic holiday. And during that same French portion of his remarks, he was asked if he can take a moment to even contemplate how far the Spurs have come. Short answer: no. “I have absolutely no desire to do that right now,” he said.

item.title: Wembanyama is not Muslim,
item.description: Maxime Ausin: I’m seeing a lot of misinformation about Victor Wembanyama’s pregame outfit, even from major American media. @hugo_levay asked the player directly, and Wemby confirmed it was an outfit for Eid al-Adha, the Muslim celebration happening this week, not a monk’s robe. PS: Wembanyama is not Muslim, at least, the last time I asked him, he said he didn’t identify with any particular religion.

item.title: As multiple general managers told The Athletic long …
item.description: To use the Memphis example above, some asked why the restriction wouldn’t be that Utah couldn’t land in the top five in 2027 regardless of whose pick it owned. Surely the rule was never designed to stop the Grizzlies from picking there. As multiple general managers told The Athletic long before this rule was adopted, however, this created asymmetries in the market that likely would have resulted in teams of roughly equal talent just trading picks with each other. Teams could also sidestep trade

item.title: That would leave Peterson the most probable selection …
item.description: For many of the scouts on lottery teams, conversations have centered on KU’s Darryn Peterson. Despite a tumultuous and turbulent season, Peterson remains in contention for the No. 1 overall pick. Still, BYU’s AJ Dybantsa remains the favorite to be picked atop the draft. That would leave Peterson the most probable selection at No. 2, as several NBA scouts and draft evaluators have told The Star he’s unlikely to fall beyond that. To understand how the NBA Draft’s top picks could shake out — and ho
