item.description: Ballislife.com: Giannis's advice: "Make sure your lawyer, financial advisor and agent should not know each other." "Rich is what you see. Wealth is what you don't see. Extreme wealth is what you don't see, you have, haven't spent, and kept investing." "You money doubles every 7 years if you invested in the market. Even if I don't do nothing, I'll probably be a billionaire while I'm playing." "Speak things into existence because your brain listens to you and once your brain listens to you, it doe

item.title: Nikola Jokic and Giannis Antetokounmpo made headlines …
item.description: While the Denver Nuggets and Milwaukee Bucks are following the 2026 NBA Playoffs, presented by Google, from afar, Nikola Jokic and Giannis Antetokounmpo made headlines from the VIP section of the 2026 UEFA Champions League Final in Budapest, Hungary, on Saturday. The Serbian superstar, a devoted Arsenal supporter, broke his typical stoic demeanor, going viral after broadcast cameras caught him celebrating intensely when Kai Havertz opened the scoring for the Gunners. The Joker has long credited

item.title: Kuzma: I will be breaking my silence soon & going on …
item.description: Kyle Kuzma: I will be breaking my silence soon & going on TBPN to discuss the future of AI, autonomous defense, robotics and biotech and energy infrastructure @sama, let me know if you want to meet after. We are still in the early innings of American Dynamism. Much to discuss.

item.title: Blazers star Jerami Grant is currently in Saudi Arabia …
item.description: Portland Trail Blazers star Jerami Grant is currently in Saudi Arabia 🇸🇦 to perform Hajj. May God accept from him ❤️

item.title: Spurs expected to decline Julian Champagnie's team option, re-sign him to better deal
item.description: Brian Windhorst on Julian Champagnie: Pretty good claim by the Spurs. They also signed him to an excellent contract which has another year on it as a team option. And I think they're going to decline his option and then re-sign him to a better contract.

item.title: Green doubles down that the Knicks need a true 1A …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Draymond Green doubles down that the Knicks need a true 1A player to win a championship: “Just like Becky Hammon said, prove me wrong. Absolutely double down. Getting out of the East has never been a sure fire thing to winning a championship. What y’all talking about? You’re supposed to get out of the East. It’s the f*cking East!”

item.title: Holmgren only took three total 3s the last four games …
item.description: Anthony Slater: Chet Holmgren only took three total 3s the last four games of the Spurs series. His 3-point volume has been decreasing. Mark Daigneault said it's a "priority" to correct that. But "Every minute Chet Holmgren has been on the team we've been the 1-seed in the Western Conference."

item.title: Mark Daigneault on Jalen Williams’ injury-related …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Mark Daigneault on Jalen Williams’ injury-related absences this season and the importance of his summer “Missing him impacts your ceiling. Missing Ajay Mitchell impacts your ceiling. But we've been able to maintain a really high floor. I'm proud of that. That's a huge accomplishment for the season, and it will pay dividends moving forward and will pay dividends for us this year. “I'm excited about a healthy Jalen Williams. I'm excited about a healthy team and what that can look lik

item.title: Most points by a player age 20 or younger in a single …
item.description: Polymarket Hoops: Most points by a player age 20 or younger in a single playoff run: 351 — Jayson Tatum (2018) 335 — Tyler Herro (2020) 293 — Magic Johnson (1980) 235 — Dylan Harper (2026)

item.title: Draymond Green says players who flop, don’t win …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Draymond Green says players who flop, don’t win championships: “Guys that flop, don’t win. You just got floppers out there, they don’t win. So y’all can stop calling SGA a flopper, because he’s a champion. I can tell you floppers don’t really win. It just don’t happen. Worried about them flopping? Play better defense. If not, shut up.” (Via @DraymondShow)

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander on not playing the NBA Finals: 'It was a failure'
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander has set the bar for what he values in this league pretty high. How does he measure a season where OKC fell short of the Finals? "It was a failure. I did not achieve what I wanted to achieve. In my experiences I learn the most about myself and make the most gains when I fail"

item.title: SGA on not having Jalen Williams: "Not having Dub for …
item.description: Brandon Rahbar: SGA on not having Jalen Williams: "Not having Dub for the whole year basically sucked. He's such a unicorn out there. He's a 20 point per game scorer, guards 1-5. Last season he got All NBA for offense, All NBA for defense and was an All Star. At 24 years old. Losing a guy like that hurts. Having a swiss army knife like that.. is a luxury.. and we didn't have that luxury this year."

item.title: Daigneault on the opportunity for Jalen Williams to …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Mark Daigneault on the opportunity for Jalen Williams to get healthy this summer: "I'm excited about a healthy Jalen Williams. I'm excited about a healthy team and what that can look like moving forward. So that becomes the priority, especially with him. It was a tough year for him, and we need to do everything we can to get him in full form, and he does too. I give the big guy a ton of credit. He played the entire playoff run last year with a ligament tear in his wrist that no

item.title: Simmons on the Pelicans trading Trey Murphy: “We …
item.description: Garland Gillen: Bill Simmons on the Pelicans trading Trey Murphy: “We assume he’s probably going to get traded over the next three weeks. I think Boston is a possibility. We thought whoever lost this matchup (Spurs and Thunder) is a possibility. You have two to three weeks to trade for someone like that.”

item.title: Luguentz Dort on upcoming 2026-27 team option: 'My main goal is to stay here'
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Lu Dort on his upcoming team option: "The conversation hasn’t happened yet. I have a lot of trust in this organization, in Sam (Presti). Really grateful for all the stuff he did for me to this point. I want to stay here. This organization, this city shaped me as a player. My main goal is to stay here."

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander on trusting Sam Presti to make …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander on trusting Sam Presti to make the right decisions this summer: "I don't have the time or energy to do Sam's job in my everyday life. Basketball takes up a lot. You can't wear every hat in every relationship. I can't go home and be the wife of the house and the kid of the house. I just can't do that. I've got to wear my dad hat. It's the same thing when I come to work. I wear the Shai hat. Sam wears the Sam hat. Mark wears the Mark hat. Chet wears the Che

item.title: Fans hoping to attend Game 1 of the NBA Finals between …
item.description: Fans hoping to attend Game 1 of the NBA Finals between the San Antonio Spurs and New York Knicks will need deep pockets, with resale ticket prices topping $1,200.

item.title: The cheapest options were located in the arena's …
item.description: According to a Ticketmaster listing, the lowest available resale tickets were listed at approximately $1,245 per seat as of Sunday. The cheapest options were located in the arena's upper-level seating sections, while premium seats were listed for more than $4,300 each before taxes.

item.title: We’re learning more details after a 17-year-old was …
item.description: We’re learning more details after a 17-year-old was hospitalized in critical condition after falling from a vehicle while celebrating a Spurs win. The teen, identified by a family member as Joey Rodriguez, remains in the hospital on life support as his doctors continue to run tests.

item.title: Yvonne Hudson, a family member of Joey's, says he was …
item.description: Yvonne Hudson, a family member of Joey's, says he was out celebrating with a few close friends when he fell from the vehicle after it hit a curb. His friends picked up him and rushed him to the nearest hospital before EMS arrived. He was later transferred to University Hospital with severe brain swelling.

item.title: Alex Caruso: I'm looking forward to being in Oklahoma City a few more years
item.description: Justin Martinez: Alex Caruso on his time with OKC so far: "Obviously I signed a long-term contract, so I'm looking forward to being here a few more years. The relationship has been great to me and the Thunder organization. I'm thankful to Sam (Presti) for everything, giving me an extension and trust me enough to be there for this handful of years. We'll see what the future holds next time. I've enjoyed my first two years here. It's been great.“

item.title: Alex Caruso on when he felt San Antonio could be a …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Alex Caruso on when he felt San Antonio could be a real threat to OKC: "I didn't speculate just because so much happens in the course of the season, you know? One injury changes the team's entire season. (It's) the reason the Spurs got Dylan Harper. Wemby missed a whole bunch of games with the leg stuff. Sometimes you need the course of NBA seasons to shape up and see what the future is going to be. You can't always predict that."

item.title: Isaiah Hartenstein on Knicks: 'I'm just rooting for them right now'
item.description: Justin Martinez: Isaiah Hartenstein on his former team, New York, competing in the NBA Finals: "Happy for them. ... I'd rather be playing against them, but I'm just rooting for them right now. There's a lot of guys I know. There's a lot of guys I'm pretty close with. So just happy for them."

item.title: Becky Hammon on the Spurs making the NBA Finals: …
item.description: Kendra Andrews: Becky Hammon on the Spurs making the NBA Finals: “Sweet, it's awesome…It takes a lot of people doing a lot of things right for to build that. That doesn't just happen overnight…. Obviously, you don't just have a franchise guy in Wemby, you have a generational guy. It’s been really fun to watch them play”

item.title: Josh Hart on how he reacts to centers — and, more …
item.description: Fred Katz: Josh Hart on how he reacts to centers — and, more specifically, Victor Wembanyama — guarding him so they can roam into the lane: "The only unanimous Defensive Player of the Year is guarding me, so that's a good sign for me, right? ... I'm a pretty good player."

item.title: Deuce McBride on the Spurs' young guards: "They're …
item.description: Fred Katz: Deuce McBride on the Spurs' young guards: "They're relentless. They're young. ... (They) just go out there and make it a tough game. I love that."

item.title: Isaiah Hartenstein on his future with OKC: Whatever happens, I'm truly grateful
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Isaiah Hartenstein, who has a team option incoming: “I love being here, I love the organization, but a lot of it's in their hands. It's a business at the end of the day, but again, just whatever happens, (I’m) truly grateful.”

item.title: Isaiah Hartenstein notes that this Thunder roster once …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Isaiah Hartenstein notes that this Thunder roster once represented an underdog story — with Lu Dort being undrafted, Hartenstein spending time in the G League, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander being traded — but says “it’s a little different now, I guess.” What does he mean? “I mean it’s…next question?”

item.title: Alex Caruso “Ran out of time. We weren’t good enough …
item.description: Rylan Stiles: Alex Caruso “Ran out of time. We weren’t good enough in stretches, we didn’t course correct fast enough. Game 1 and 7, we had chances to win both games. We made mistakes, they hit really tough shots as well.”

item.title: Lu Dort on how much he has grown as a person during …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Lu Dort on how much he has grown as a person during his time with OKC: "A lot. Just imagining me at 20 years old when I came here first, the person I am today is totally different. Just the way that this organization shapes you on the court and off the court as a man, how to be professional and also just the love that we get from this city and our fans, there's nowhere I go around the city where I don't get reminded that I'm a Thunder player. People are so happy to see me. It's bee

item.title: Isaac McKneely has an NBA pre-draft workout with the …
item.description: Vince Wolfram: Louisville’s Isaac McKneely has an NBA pre-draft workout with the Brooklyn Nets on Monday, per sources. The 6-4 guard from Poca, West Virginia averaged 10.9 PTS, 3.0 REB, and 1.4 AST while shooting 39.5% from three this season after transferring from Virginia.

item.title: Jalen Williams on facing San Antonio: 'It honestly feels like we are playing a version of ourselves'
item.description: Anthony Slater: Jalen Williams on the Spurs: “It honestly feels like we are playing a version of ourselves.” Could he have changed the outcome? “I think we could’ve won if I played…But it’s also hats off to them. What do you want them to do about me being hurt?”

item.title: Jalen Williams on his hamstring injuries, the timing …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Jalen Williams on his hamstring injuries, the timing of his return, what this stretch taught him. “We went through all the right protocol to try and give it a go to play. … Everybody in the world knows I wasn't myself. I played nine minutes (in Game 6), I don't even think I shot the ball, I just couldn't move, but I'd rather have went out there and tested it out.”

item.title: Chet Holmgren on how he processes losing throughout …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Chet Holmgren on how he processes losing throughout his basketball career, where he has won so much at every level: "I'd definitely say they don't suck any less. Losing isn't fun, but I definitely feel like I've gotten better at being able to kind of take a loss for what it is and look at it objectively rather than emotionally and try to figure it out and work forward from there."

item.title: Chet Holmgren on how he plans to approach the …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Chet Holmgren on how he plans to approach the offseason after not playing to his standard against San Antonio: "I feel like it comes down to looking at the tape, figuring out where I can improve individually and then trusting my work through the offseason."

item.title: Mitchell Robinson practices individually, status for Finals Game 1 still TBD
item.description: Ian Begley: Mike Brown said Mitchell Robinson did individual stuff in practice today. Obviously, he is playing with his hand protected. Brown did not have a Game 1 status for Robinson. Robinson will certainly push to play in the game

item.title: Chet Holmgren reflects on his struggles in the Spurs …
item.description: Anthony Slater: Chet Holmgren reflects on his struggles in the Spurs series: “I feel like there were definitely opportunities to get more attempts up that I didn't in the moment. That's an area to improve.”

item.title: Thomas Sorber plans to play Summer League after missing full 2025-26 season
item.description: Justin Martinez: Thomas Sorber on if the plan is for him to play in Summer League: "That would be my plan, but I'm not sure on my timeline yet."

item.title: Jaylin Williams on being no longer just being viewed …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Jaylin Williams on being no longer just being viewed as "the other J. Williams": “I'm not seeking approval from anybody. I mean, it's just people talking. It doesn't matter to me what they call me. I've been called way worse in my life. I've been called way worse the past 12 hours. But I don't seek approval. I don't really care what they call me. My teammates call me J Will. My family calls me Jay. People I love, they call me by my name. At the end of the day, that's all that ma

item.title: Nikola Topic on what he learned about himself this …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Nikola Topic on what he learned about himself this season after overcoming testicular cancer and making his return to play: "I learned a lot about myself. I didn't know how tough I was, honestly. It wasn't the best, but I'm grateful that I went through those experiences. I grew from those experiences as well, and it made me a better person today. I wouldn't be here if those things didn't happen. I'm looking forward to getting ready to play."

item.title: Kings announce pre-draft workout for Monday in …
item.description: Sean Cunningham: Kings announce pre-draft workout for Monday in Sacramento with: #1 Quadir Copeland (North Carolina State), G/F; #2 Melvin Council (Kansas), G; #3 Tre White (Kansas), G; #4 Nick Martinelli (Northwestern), F; #5 Keshawn Murphy (Auburn), F; #6 Caden Powell (Baylor)

item.title: Cason Wallace on potential extension: Whether I get paid this summer or not, I'm going to get better
item.description: Justin Martinez: Cason Wallace on being up for a contract extension this summer: "I love the Thunder. But I'm more focused on getting better this summer and being able to go back out there and compete. I'm not money driven. I want to compete, that's the main focus of me. So whether I get paid this summer or not, you know, I'm going to get better because that's what I have to do to stay in this league."

item.title: Chet Holmgren on how much he'll tailor his offseason …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Chet Holmgren on how much he'll tailor his offseason toward preparing for the Spurs: "I definitely think it's different in terms of I don't think there's another team that has their play style and their personnel. So they're unique in that way. You can't just kind of play like a base normal, you know, this is what we do on an average Tuesday night type of thing. The way you have to attack it is look at the film, look at where you can improve and then work on the things that you

item.title: The Brunsons will become the first father-son duo to …
item.description: NBA Communications: Rick Brunson played for the Knicks in the 1999 NBA Finals. Jalen Brunson will do the same in 2026. The Brunsons will become the first father-son duo to each play in the NBA Finals for the same franchise. Game 1: Wednesday, June 3 at 8:30 PM ET on ABC.

item.title: Josh Hart, one of the smartest players in the league, …
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Josh Hart, one of the smartest players in the league, was asked today at Knicks practice about what the team can learn from playing the Spurs in the NBA Cup final. "Technically, that game didn't happen," Hart said. (It didn't count in season standings or stats, so he's right.)

item.title: Mike Brown said Mitchell Robinson has to be cleared by …
item.description: Stefan Bondy: Mike Brown said Mitchell Robinson has to be cleared by the medical staff before "he can go out there."

item.title: For those wondering, the NBA Finals does not have a …
item.description: Sports Media Watch: For those wondering, the NBA Finals does not have a presenting sponsor this year. (Had previously been YouTube TV, which was the first presenting sponsor ever for the event.)

item.title: NBA Finals court designs will feature the Larry O'Brien Trophy
item.description: Matt Guzman: Here's a look at the courts both teams will use for the NBA Finals, affixed with the Larry O'Brien Trophy at the center. This looks awesome. #PorVida

item.title: For one bettor on DraftKings Sportsbook, however, a …
item.description: The Knicks finally know who their opponent will be following Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs’ monumental Game 7 closeout win over the Thunder on Saturday night. For one bettor on DraftKings Sportsbook, however, a $49,000 straight bet on the Knicks to win the NBA Finals for a massive $980,000 payout still hangs in the balance. The finals winner bet was made a day after the Knicks beat the Hawks in Round 1 on May 1, as they were priced with 19-to-1 odds to win the championship.

item.title: “The LeBron side, from what I am told, would like the …
item.description: The situation comes as the Lakers continue building around Luka Doncic while attempting to retain key pieces. According to ESPN insider Brian Windhorst, James’ camp wants clarity from the Lakers before any decision is made. “The LeBron side, from what I am told, would like the Lakers to come and say, ‘LeBron, here’s our plan for you. Here’s what we’re offering,’” Windhorst said on “The Hoop Collective.” “And if they’re offering less than the max, here’s why: ‘Because we’re going to use this cap

item.title: Kenrich Williams would like to retire with OKC
item.description: Rylan Stiles: Kenrich Williams “It’s no secret that I want to be here until I’m done playing.” Added “Just the environment, the culture, the people that I work with every day. This is a job where I can come in and be myself and everybody around me is good people and a good environment.”

item.title: “I mean, that was December. I don't think that's …
item.description: Josh Hart, in fact, took the comedic route in explaining why there's nothing for the Knicks to glean from that game. “I mean, that was December. I don't think that's really gonna be equivalent to what the atmosphere and energy is going to be like,” Hart said, via SNY. “Technically, that game didn't happen, so I don't think there's anything that we can learn from.”

item.title: “Every minute Chet Holmgren’s been on the team, we’ve …
item.description: After the series concluded, several people were asked about Holmgren’s production. Thunder head coach Mark Daigneault made it very clear that OKC is better with Holmgren. “Every minute Chet Holmgren’s been on the team, we’ve been the 1 seed in the Western Conference,” Daigneault said Sunday, when the team gathered for end-of-season meetings. “And it wasn’t the case before Chet was healthy.” During their exit press conferences Sunday, Holmgren met with the media to assess his season. Of course, h

item.title: "I thought it was over, I ain't going to lie to you," …
item.description: Julian Champagnie's improbable journey from being unceremoniously cut by Philadelphia on Valentine's Day 2023 to reaching the NBA Finals as a key member of the Spurs came to mind as he held onto the ball at the end of Game 7 of the Western Conference Finals. "I thought it was over, I ain't going to lie to you," the forward said, recalling how emotional he was when the Sixers let him go as a 21-year-old undrafted rookie out of St. John's.

item.title: "My teammates do a really good job of keeping me in …
item.description: Stephen Curry is the only player in NBA history with more 3-pointers in a conference finals Game 7. "My teammates do a really good job of keeping me in check and making sure I don't get too high and never get too low, so I give all that to them honestly and truly," he said. "That's something I've been working on for a long time now, trying to stay even keeled. I've had my ups and downs with it, but I feel like this year my teammates really did pull me together and tell me, 'We need you, so never

item.title: "Who are your top three defensive teammates of all …
item.description: Draymond Green: "Who are your top three defensive teammates of all time? Andrew Bogut number one, Klay Thompson number two. Klay Thompson, ball hawk. And I'd say number three, number three, my favorite defensive teammate of all time, or not favorite, but top three. Kevin Durant was elite defensively here. Elite. Andre Iguodala. Andre Iguodala made the game really easy. Got to go with Andre. It has to be Andre."

item.title: Scott Perry met with the family of Houston point guard …
item.description: ‪Matt George‬: Per a league source, Sacramento Kings general manager Scott Perry met with the family of Houston point guard Kingston Flemings this weekend in San Antonio. Flemings is expected to be amongst the guards potentially available for the Kings with the 7th pick in the NBA Draft.

item.title: “They definitely want to beat me and I want to kick …
item.description: Now, Brown, 56, is heading back to a city where his family still resides, needing to defeat the friends and the franchise that helped catapult his career to claim his first championship as a head coach. “They definitely want to beat me and I want to kick their ass,” Brown said following Sunday’s practice. “You love ’em and you can always love ’em before and after … I got ties to San Antonio and you appreciate the people, you appreciate the journey and all that other stuff. But at the end of the

item.title: He got rid of a lot of the people that had connections …
item.description: Brian Berger on Blazers owner Tom Dundon: "He's been a bull in a china shop so far. He's come in and just cut costs. He didn't allow t-shirts. I look at teams like the Knicks and the Spurs and you see all the former players sitting courtside and how the teams have embraced the former players. He got rid of a lot of the people that had connections to the former players last week when they laid off 70 people. He's cutting hotel costs. He's running this kind of like he runs the Carolina Hurricanes,

item.title: Brian Berger on Blazers owner Tom Dundon: "I think …
item.description: Brian Berger on Blazers owner Tom Dundon: "I think he's going to do everything in his power to move them. It breaks my heart to say that because I used to work for the Trail Blazers. I live in Portland. But this seems to be Sonics 2.0. I think he's going to say all the right things and at the end of the day, he's going to come with a pretty cutthroat deal for the state and the city. And when they don't make that deal, I think he's going to use that as his excuse to move the team. The way it work

item.title: Kendrick Perkins: Rich Paul hurting LeBron’s GOAT argument
item.description: NBA Courtside: Kendrick Perkins believes Rich Paul is actually HURTING LeBron’s GOAT argument by his podcast takes: “This is where he has to stop. This is where he starts to hurt LeBron James GOAT case. This is where people start saying the ‘controlling of the narrative.’ You speaking on MJ and saying these things and it’s like ‘here go Rich Paul, LeBron’s guy to hype up his GOAT status.’ But it’s not hyping it up, it’s actually making it worse!”

item.title: Clippers open to trading down in the draft?
item.description: But Presti rarely settles. The Thunder are sitting on a mountain of future picks and approaching a major financial crossroads. Some consolidation is going to be necessary. League sources widely expect the Clippers to listen to trade-down offers for the fifth pick. So maybe there’s a deal to put together with 12, 17, and a player to get there. And then from the fifth pick, can OKC build an overwhelming offer to get up into the top three? Could that deal include a 24-year-old All-NBA player?

item.title: The Thunder also hold the 12th and 17th picks in a …
item.description: The Thunder also hold the 12th and 17th picks in a loaded draft, and there have been rumblings for weeks that they want to package them and move up — some have reported Oklahoma City is targeting Duke power forward Cam Boozer. But Boozer projects to go in the top three, which means leaping from 12 to the front of the lottery. A more realistic target might be 7-foot-3 Michigan center Aday Mara. Or maybe they keep one pick and flip the other for a future first to keep the assets coming.

item.title: Rich Paul says he always viewed LeBron as the …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Rich Paul says he always viewed LeBron as the ‘People’s champ’ like Muhammad Ali: “If you win 1 time in Cleveland, there’s nothing else to be done… The idea of going back home and winning one. I always looked at LeBron to be more of what I would say the Peoples champ like Muhammad Ali was. Even when he lost I feel like people really embraced him.”

item.title: A stadium full of Seattle sports fans at the Mariners …
item.description: A stadium full of Seattle sports fans at the Mariners game cheering on and celebrating watching the Thunder lose

item.title: Austin Reaves a bigger priority for Lakers than LeBron James?
item.description: The Los Angeles Lakers have a pair of prominent free agents in LeBron James and Austin Reaves, and who they ultimately prioritize this summer may come as a surprise to some, as NBA reporter Jovan Buha elaborated upon on Friday. "There's two big differences between LeBron and Reaves right now. One, LeBron is a short-term solution. Reaves is viewed by the franchise as a long-term cornerstone. So if it comes down to, would you rather pay Reaves $40 million for the next five years, or LeBron $40 mil

item.title: "Yes, they can keep Austin Reaves if they were to sign …
item.description: Jovan Buha: "Yes, they can keep Austin Reaves if they were to sign Jalen Duren. I continue to say that Austin Reaves' contract situation is separate from the Lakers' cap space. Now, technically it relates to the cap space in the sense of if Austin walks, the Lakers would get an additional 21 million in cap space, but you are not replacing Austin Reaves with 21 million dollars. So, assuming that they keep him on the cap hold and re-sign him to some number above that, which again, I think is the w

item.title: Derek Harper expects Luka Doncic to retire with the Mavericks
item.description: Derek Harper: "I love Nico, Nico is my buddy, but I would not want my name on that trade if I ever had anything to do with the NBA. You look back, you gave away what, 30 points, eight and nine, you gave it to somebody else for a guy that is no longer on your roster. That one is hard to get around. It is just something at the time I think they thought about doing and they were quick to react, and it just did not work out, especially here in Dallas. People love Luka here. I think he will eventuall

item.title: “Donovan has been steadfast in how much he loves it …
item.description: Eligible for a contract extension on July 7, Cleveland Cavaliers All-Star guard Donovan Mitchell is highly coveted by the organization. With one year and a player option left on his current deal, Cavs president Koby Altman was asked about his status and a potential agreement, whether this summer or next offseason. “Donovan has been steadfast in how much he loves it here, and so there hasn't been any question of will he be here and does he want to be here,” Altman said. “So I think that's the big

item.title: Multiple Cavaliers executives would oppose Giannis Antetokounmpo trade involving Evan Mobley
item.description: The problem: The Cavs love Mobley, they believe in him and there are multiple members of the Cleveland brain trust that would be vehemently against a Giannis-Mobley swap, as the 2025 Defensive Player of the Year and Second-Team All-NBA selection is viewed as not only a future star but also a proverbial safety net when it comes to another rebuild. There’s also the matter of Antetokounmpo’s contract. Would he be willing to extend in Cleveland beyond the one guaranteed season left on his current de

item.title: The 2026 Oklahoma City Thunder had the second best …
item.description: ‪Lev Akabas‬: The 2026 Oklahoma City Thunder had the second best regular season net rating of any NBA team that didn't win the title

item.title: RJ Barrett is in attendance for today’s press …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Rowan Barrett, GM of the senior men’s team, and the program’s new head coach Gordie Herbert are announcing Canada Basketball’s player pool for this summer’s FIBA World Cup qualifiers. RJ Barrett is in attendance for today’s press conference.

item.title: Sean Sweeney has officially been named Orlando’s next …
item.description: ‪Jason Beede‬: New: Sean Sweeney has officially been named Orlando’s next head coach, the #Magic announced. Sweeney will stick with the Spurs through the Finals. “Sean brings a tremendous work ethic and a high degree of intensity that set the tone for everything he does,” Jeff Weltman said.

item.title: He agreed to a four-year deal with the Magic that …
item.description: Sweeney, 41, will continue with the Spurs through the NBA Finals, the Magic said. San Antonio has homecourt advantage when it takes on the New York Knicks in the Finals, which start Wednesday night on ABC. He agreed to a four-year deal with the Magic that includes an additional one-year team option, a league source confirmed to the Orlando Sentinel. Sports Illustrated first reported the length of Sweeney’s contract.

item.title: Julius Erving: 'I think LeBron is one of one. Kobe’s another sort of imitation of Michael Jordan'
item.description: Now, the “Doctor” has added another controversial take to his long list – he’s picking LeBron over Kobe Bryant. And what’s controversial there isn’t who he picked but why he chose him. “I think LeBron is one of one,” said Erving when asked by “The Schmo”. “Kobe’s another sort of imitation of Michael Jordan. You know, there have been plenty of imitations of Michael, Kobe, me and other people. LeBron’s like one of one. So I’m going to give him this pick.”

item.title: Every player in the 2026 NBA Finals will wear a USA …
item.description: Topps: BREAKING: Every player in the 2026 NBA Finals will wear a USA 250 patch on their jersey. The patches will be removed after each game, with select game-worn patches later featured inside ultra-rare trading cards 🔥

item.title: Here's Canada Basketball's full player pool for this …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Here's Canada Basketball's full player pool for this summer's World Cup qualifiers. They'll face Puerto Rico on July 3 and Jamaica on July 6, both in Hamilton, as well as another game (opponent TBD) in Quebec City on Aug. 31.

item.description: Josh Lewenberg: To sum up NBAers in for Canada this summer: SGA, NAW, Barrett, Brooks, Dort, Nembhard bros, Mathurin, Olynyk, George, Miller Out this summer but in for this cycle: Edey, Powell Didn’t commit: Murray, Sharpe, Wiggins Committed but not selected: Lawson, Banton, Houstan, Prosper

item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Rowan Barrett was asked about Jamal Murray, who's absent from Canada's player pool for this cycle. "No, Jamal Murray has not committed to playing moving forward." item.title: Scripps is among the largest operators of ABC …
item.description: Two months since Scripps affiliates went dark for Comcast customers, the local station group’s broadcast networks have now gone dark for DirecTV customers across the country. Scripps is among the largest operators of ABC affiliates across the country, meaning impacted customers will be unable to watch the NBA Finals or Stanley Cup Final — both of which start this week — through DirecTV platforms.

item.title: Jayson Williams, who was previously incarcerated on a …
item.description: Former NBA star Jayson Williams, who was previously incarcerated on a gun charge, will be one of the featured speakers in the first-ever gun safety course in the U.S. to be run at a jail, Sheriff Errol Toulon said Monday. Williams will be one of the featured presenters in the month-long course, along with firearm safety advocates, people directly affected by gun violence, and community groups.

item.title: Jeremy Sochan on how to get Victor Wembanyama tired: Give him different looks, be very physical and make him run
item.description: Jeremy Sochan signed with the Knicks after clearing waivers, and now his new team will face off against his old team in the NBA Finals. “Whether I’m playing or not, it’s important to feed all the information I have. And I think I know quite a lot,” Sochan said in an exclusive interview with Jared Schwartz of the New York Post. “Being that tall [Wembanyama] gets tired,” Sochan continued. “Give him different looks and [not beat him up but] be very physical, and make him run. And he’s gonna get tir

item.title: He said he sold around 2,000 shirts during the 2014 …
item.description: Others skipped the wait by shopping with vendors set up along Southwest Military. Garry Ortega, who has been selling merchandise for about 40 years, said demand is already outpacing the Spurs’ last Finals run. He said he sold around 2,000 shirts during the 2014 Spurs Final season, and that this year he has already sold about 4,000. Ortega said, “Wemby is the number one seller of course.”

item.title: In the hours after the win, some fans headed to …
item.description: In the hours after the win, some fans headed to Academy Sports for Spurs Finals gear. The store opened its doors after the victory to long lines of customers eager to buy Finals T-shirts and hats. Academy had boxes of pre-printed items ready to go, and one fan who waited about half an hour to reach the front of the line said, “Im glad we got here when we did cause there's already a line to get in so we just beat the line to get in the store its ok we love our Spurs.”

item.title: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is making sure that New York City …
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is making sure that New York City schools students can stay up late to watch the Knicks play in the NBA Finals. The mayor was joined by a group of students Monday morning to sign an executive order that repeals bedtime so the kids can stay up late to watch the Knicks. "Bedtime is repealed! All of you can watch the Finals," the mayor declared.

item.title: Hornets guarantee Moussa Diabate's contract for 2026-27
item.description: Ohm Youngmisuk: The Charlotte Hornets have guaranteed Moussa Diabate’s contract for next season according to league sources. It was non-guaranteed. Diabate won the NBA Hustle Award this season.

item.title: Spurs-Thunder Game 6 drew largest audience of the season for an NBA broadcast
item.description: Richard Deitsch: NBC Sports said Game 6 between Spurs-Thunder averaged 11.6 million viewers on NBC and Peacock. Most-watched game of the season which will surely be topped by Game 7. Blue Jays-Mariners Game 7 on Fox properties did 9.1 million viewers; Daytona 500 this year did 7.49M; CFP semis averaged 16.8M on ESPN Networks if you want some random comps.

item.title: San Antonio police arrested two suspects along …
item.description: San Antonio police arrested two suspects along Southwest Military Drive after the Spurs won the Western Conference Championship on Saturday night. The first incident happened around midnight Sunday. According to a preliminary report, one suspect, an unidentified 42-year-old man, was seen jumping on vehicles.

item.title: Officers responded to a fight approximately an hour …
item.description: Officers responded to a fight approximately an hour after the first incident. The suspect, identified as Francis Cervantes, 29, was told to move her vehicle. When Cervantes reversed her vehicle, SAPD said she struck a parked vehicle. She eventually pulled over and struck an officer, police said. The officer had no physical injuries, according to a preliminary report.

item.title: Sean Elliott: Victor Wembanyama hasn't really begun to reach his true potential
item.description: Sean Elliott on Victor Wembanyama: At 22 years old, he's still getting there. I still think he hasn't really begun to reach his true potential. I don't think he's even close, actually. Dan, I feel like he's going to fill out a lot more. He's going to continue to figure out the game a lot more. He's going to continue to work on his game because that's the kind of person he is and that's the kind of basketball player he wants to be. You saw what he did with the monks in China last summer. He worke

item.title: Lou Williams says the Thunder shouldn't give up on …
item.description: Run It Back: Lou Williams says the Thunder shouldn't give up on Chet Holmgren. "Lu Dort will be the adjustment." Chandler Parsons: "Good news for OKC is Hartenstein & Dort are their only free agents. Bad news is Harrison Barnes is the only FA on the Spurs."

item.title: Big Perk expects the Spurs-Knicks series to go 7 games …
item.description: ESPN: Big Perk expects the Spurs-Knicks series to go 7 games 🔥🏀 “I expect another seven game series. This is David and Goliath.”

item.title: Miami, Portland in pursuit of Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: More and more, with sources saying Miami and Portland are already in pursuit, Antetokounmpo's future is increasingly expected to be resolved over the next few weeks in conjunction with NBA Draft proceedings.

item.title: There is a growing belief leaguewide that the Nuggets …
item.description: There is a growing belief leaguewide that the Nuggets will explore their potential trade options with Christian Braun as well as Cam Johnson in a bid to create financial flexibility for matching offers to restricted free agent Peyton Watson and perhaps further retooling of Nikola Jokić's supporting cast.

item.title: It's believed that Elton Brand, who worked as GM under …
item.description: Sixers assistant general manager Jameer Nelson is widely expected to be promoted to GM under Gansey, who will replace Daryl Morey and report for Harris Blitzer Sports & Entertainment president Bob Myers. It's believed that Elton Brand, who worked as GM under Morey, will be offered another position within the organization.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama on thriving under pressure: The crazy thing is I want to do it 15, 20 more times, maybe it's an addiction
item.description: Now that he’s been pushed beyond his breaking point and found another Wemby on the other side of it, he’s reveling in the new world he’s unlocked for himself. The desperation he found with his back against the wall showed that there are still new Victors to discover deep down inside. “The crazy thing is, maybe I’m crazy for that, but I want to do that 15, 20 more times,” Wembanyama said. “Let’s hope it doesn’t become an addiction. Maybe it is already.”

item.title: Miami Heat point guard Davion Mitchell is training at …
item.description: Iacopo De Santis: Miami Heat point guard Davion Mitchell is training at the Varese Campus in Italy as a guest of the Italian team Openjobmetis Varese. He averaged 9.3 points and 6.5 assists per game during the latest NBA season.

item.title: Per league source, the Pistons held predraft workouts …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Per league source, the Pistons held predraft workouts today including the following players: Nick Boyd (Wisconsin) Rafael Castro (George Washington) Nate Johnson (Kansas State) Xavian Lee (Florida)

item.title: After more than a year of analyzing Dallas sites for …
item.description: After more than a year of analyzing Dallas sites for their planned multi-billion-dollar arena and entertainment district, the Mavericks have chosen Valley View, dealing a major setback to efforts to bring the project downtown. The Mavericks on Monday executed option agreements to purchase approximately 104 acres of the 110-acre former site of Valley View Mall, the franchise told The Dallas Morning News in an emailed statement.

item.title: Multiple people close to Mavericks governor Dumont say …
item.description: Multiple people close to Mavericks governor Dumont say he grew increasingly leery of becoming the face of a tear-down-City Hall movement, especially since it wasn’t the Mavericks’ idea in the first place. Along with availability, multiple people close to negotiations said the Mavericks had logistical concerns about the City Hall site’s ingress and egress on game and concert nights.

item.title: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is making sure that New York City …
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is making sure that New York City schools students can stay up late to watch the Knicks play in the NBA Finals. The mayor was joined by a group of students Monday morning to sign an executive order that repeals bedtime so the kids can stay up late to watch the Knicks.

item.title: Rick Adelman (1946-2026)
item.description: NBA Coaches Assoc.: The membership of the National Basketball Coaches Association joins the NBA family in mourning the passing of legendary Head Coach and Hall of Famer, Rick Adelman. Rick Adelman coached in the NBA for 29 years, serving as a Head Coach for 23 seasons with the Portland Trail Blazers, Golden State Warriors, Sacramento Kings, Houston Rockets and Minnesota Timberwolves. Over his coaching career, Rick won 1,042 games (10th all-time), was inducted into the Naismith Basketball Hall of

item.title: Just want to make sure I'm on record saying any talk …
item.description: Will Guillory: Just want to make sure I'm on record saying any talk of trading Trey Murphy is absolute insanity

item.description: NBA Communications: Game 7 between the San Antonio Spurs and Oklahoma City Thunder has surpassed 2 BILLION views across social media platforms, becoming the most-viewed NBA game EVER on social. The series now owns the TWO MOST-VIEWED NBA games EVER on social, with Game 1 reaching 1.9 billion views.

item.title: Joseph Sanberg, co-founder of green-banking company …
item.description: Baxter Holmes: Breaking: Joseph Sanberg, co-founder of green-banking company Aspiration and charged with defrauding investors out of $248M, was just sentenced to 14 years in prison. Aspiration is at the center of an NBA investigation into the Clippers for salary cap circumvention. Story coming.

item.description: Joe Mussatto: Russell Westbrook, an investor in OKC’s new multipurpose stadium and surrounding district, speaks at the groundbreaking today.

item.title: Stephen Curry signs shoe deal with Li-Ning
item.description: Shams Charania: Golden State Warriors star Stephen Curry has signed a shoe endorsement contract with Chinese company Li-Ning -- a landmark 10-year deal, sources tell ESPN. Li-Ning extends his Curry Brand venture globally and encompasses basketball products, athleisure, the ability for Curry to sign athletes under his brand, and a full golf line.

We couldn’t understand that at one point he had lost his compass, that he didn’t care how anyone felt, and that is the most important thing in such a team environment. It was no longer about anythi

item.description: Jusuf Nurkic: After that meeting, nobody cared anymore about what he was showing. He was explaining to Kevin Durant how to make a basket… explaining to him how to score is like teaching a pilot how to fly a plane. It was absurd. In the end, he had a serious alcohol problem, that is known. Maybe that also affected things, but he completely lost control with the team. He started holding one-on-one meetings just to provoke players — Beal, Allen — you would come there and not believe what he was say

I don’t know how to explain it in the best way, but whatever he wanted others to do, that’s how it was. Whoever came in through trades had to fit into that system. Until Carmelo (laughs). He kind of stepped outside the system beca

item.title: Stephen Curry on choosing Li-Ning: Playing in Dwyane Wade, Jimmy Butler sneakers helped me decide
item.description: Nick DePaula: “Throughout my sneaker free agency, I was impressed by the quality, comfort & performance of Li-Ning’s shoes. It was during that time playing in @DwyaneWade and @JimmyButler’s sneakers, that I knew Li-Ning could be the right partner to deliver on the innovation & design that I want Curry Brand to stand for.”

item.title: “But, as you know, there’s heart and there’s IQ and …
item.description: “I always remind our scouts,” Heat assistant general manager Adam Simon said, “we always talk about we have our principles of what we like, what we look for. There’s things that we always try to like. Who doesn’t like length? Who doesn’t like athleticism? Who doesn’t like positional size? “But, as you know, there’s heart and there’s IQ and there’s ability to outplay deficiencies that you might have based on size. And if you look in the history of the game, if you basically overlook something bas

item.title: Mike Brown says Mitchell Robinson did individual work …
item.description: SNY Knicks: Mike Brown says Mitchell Robinson did individual work today and Brown is waiting on the Knicks' medical staff to tell him what the next steps are

item.title: His creativity and ingenuity led his teams to 1,042 …
item.description: Warriors PR: Warriors statement on the passing of former Head Coach Rick Adelman: Rick Adelman left an indelible mark on the NBA during his nearly four decades in the league, both as a player for seven seasons and as a coach for 29 seasons, including two seasons with the Warriors (1995-97). His creativity and ingenuity led his teams to 1,042 wins during his illustrious coaching career, the 10th-most in NBA history, and earned him entry into the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame in 2021.

item.title: Melinda French Gates is joining the ownership group of …
item.description: Melinda French Gates is joining the ownership group of the Seattle Kraken as the hockey team’s investors prepare a bid to diversify into basketball.

item.title: The arrival of French Gates adds a deep-pocketed …
item.description: The arrival of French Gates adds a deep-pocketed investor as the group eyes a National Basketball Association expansion team. NBA owners voted in March to explore adding franchises in Seattle and Las Vegas, which would mark the league’s first expansion in two decades as team valuations soar. Holloway has said her group will pursue the Seattle team, aiming to bring the NBA back to the city after the Supersonics relocated to Oklahoma City in 2008 and were renamed the Thunder.

item.title: Andre Drummond: 'I am very happy for Jared McCain. I wish we kept him'
item.description: Andre Drummond: "I am very happy for Jared McCain. I wish we kept him, obviously, but I think he went to a good situation, a team that is going to help him develop and help him grow. I love the kid to death. Very, very good kid."

item.title: I cried. Nobody knows this, but I cried. I sat in my …
item.description: Andre Drummond: The first time was in Detroit, when I got traded to Cleveland. I think that was the first time I felt real sadness. I was genuinely sad because I didn’t want to leave. I felt like I had so much more to give to Detroit. Speaker 1: Yeah. I felt like I wanted to do so much more in that state. I wanted to end my career there. I’ve always said it: I wanted to play in Detroit my whole career, and I wanted to win a championship in Detroit. It was hard. It was hard. I cried. Nobody knows

item.title: According to EPM, Towns had the best defensive season …
item.description: But is the newfound elite defensive performance sustainable? Listen to enough NBA podcasts, and you’ll frequently hear the take that the Knicks have two massive defensive holes to patch: Brunson at the point and Karl-Anthony Towns in the frontcourt. So even with a trio of very good “wing-stopper” defenders in OG Anunoby, Mikal Bridges and Josh Hart, they’re sort of treading water. The critique of Brunson is fair. He’s not a black hole like Trae Young out there, and he gets his share of steals, b

item.title: There’s probably been some luck on offense too — the …
item.description: There’s probably been some luck on offense too — the Knicks are shooting an even 40 percent from three in the playoffs, up from 37.3 percent in the regular season. Still, offense and defense aren’t as separable in basketball as is commonly assumed. The Knicks are scoring a spectacular 1.32 points per possession in transition in the playoffs; they actually aren’t getting that many steals, but the ones they do get, largely from their wing defenders, have often been pick-sixes at midcourt that set

item.title: Paul Pierce: "I believe that OKC is better than the …
item.description: Paul Pierce: "I believe that OKC is better than the Spurs still at their best healthy.

item.title: Chandler Parsons on Chet Holmgren: 'I do think there is some sort of mental block when it comes to Victor Wembanyama'
item.description: Chandler Parsons on Chet Holmgren: "I do think there is some sort of mental block when it comes to Victor Wembanyama. I really do. I don't think it is like Space Jam, but I don't think he can take two shots in Game 7. You don't play 33 minutes and take two shots. I would rather live and die with going down with Ajay Mitchell out and Jalen Williams out. I would rather have him be 0 for 15 than one for two."

item.title: The past six years, home teams are 9-12 in Game 7s in …
item.description: Tim Bontemps: The past six years, home teams are 9-12 in Game 7s in the NBA. That’s a 42.8% win rate. Prior to 2020, home teams were 106-29 in Game 7s in the NBA. That’s a 78.5% win rate.

item.title: Jusuf Nurkic: Damian Lillard was a real leader. He led by example
item.description: In a positive tone, he reflected on his period in Oregon, where, according to him, there was strong chemistry within the team. “We had that great mindset, starting with Damian Lillard, who was a real leader. He led by example. I don’t know how to explain it in the best way, but whatever he wanted others to do, that’s how it was. He could be loud, but he always led by example, whether in practice or on the plane — the guy was never late.

item.title: Whoever came in through trades had to fit into that …
item.description: Jusuf Nurkic: "We never had any problems in the locker room. Whoever came in through trades had to fit into that system. Until Carmelo (laughs). He kind of stepped outside the system because he was always late, he missed almost every meeting. The coach was traumatized by that and didn’t want anyone to be late, and he didn’t have many rules, but he stuck to that one. Carmelo wasn’t like he didn’t want to come (laughs), he was like: nobody told me. If practice was at 10, he would show up at 10:45

item.title: The coaches were thinking about how he would fit in, …

He was always an incredible talent. But he didn't want to change himself or accept a smaller role on an NBA team." Former Portland Trail Blazers center Jusuf Nurkic shared his perspective on why Mario Hezonja never fully lived up to his potential in the NBA.

item.title: There is a growing anticipation that Prosper Karangwa …
item.description: There is a growing anticipation that Prosper Karangwa will elect to stay with the 76ers after the hiring of Mike Gansey as the team's new president of basketball operations. Karangwa is the Sixers' vice president of player personnel and has drawn interest in recent weeks from the Los Angeles Lakers and Dallas.

item.title: The Mavericks have chosen Valley View, dealing a major …
item.description: After more than a year of analyzing Dallas sites for their planned multi-billion-dollar arena and entertainment district, the Mavericks have chosen Valley View, dealing a major setback to efforts to bring the project downtown. The Mavericks on Monday executed option agreements to purchase approximately 104 acres of the 110-acre former site of Valley View Mall, the franchise told The Dallas Morning News in an emailed statement. It’s a seismic decision that could reshape Dallas’ sports and enterta

item.title: That timeline was a significant factor in Monday’s …
item.description: The Mavericks were seeking at least a 50-acre footprint for the project, the centerpiece being a basketball-specific arena to be completed by the time its American Airlines Center lease expires on July 28, 2031. That timeline was a significant factor in Monday’s decision, multiple people familiar with the negotiating process told The News.

item.title: Perhaps that will happen again in the NBA Finals, …
item.description: New York Knicks fans have infiltrated opposing arenas throughout their team’s storybook playoff run. With ticket prices at Madison Square Garden out of reach for many Knicks lifers, several have hit the road to get their postseason fix. Perhaps that will happen again in the NBA Finals, especially now that, according to Gametime data from Monday morning, the get-in cost for Games 1 and 2 in San Antonio is dropping while seats in MSG are growing increasingly expensive.

item.title: The 2026 NBA Finals will be ABC’s first NBA Finals …

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo: 'I was lucky enough to be selfless, to always put other people first before my own needs'
item.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo: I knew what I was worth, but at the same time, I was lucky enough to be very selfless because I was doing things for other people. If it was just for me, if I was being selfish, I would have said, “Yeah, I’ll sign that paper. My brother will sign whenever it is.” But I wasn’t that person. One of my biggest pet peeves is when people are inconsiderate of other people’s feelings, space, time and energy. God blessed me, and I was lucky enough to be selfless, to always put othe

item.title: That doesn’t allow you to learn. And if you don’t …
item.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo: When a player or an athlete starts playing, he doesn’t know about financial literacy. He doesn’t know how to save his money, invest his money — none of that — at that age. When you come at 18 in the NBA, it’s like people provide you your lawyer, your agent and your financial adviser. You come in, and it’s like, “Oh, yeah, these are your people. These are the people you’ve got to trust. These are the people that are going to handle everything in your life.” What does that d

item.title: Don’t ever let your lawyer, financial adviser and …
item.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo: The advice I would give young athletes, or anybody that’s getting into a space that he doesn’t really understand, is there are two things that you can do. One: Don’t ever let your lawyer, financial adviser and agent know one another — ever. They should never be boys and cool, man, you know? Because they keep one another accountable. If they don’t know each other, one guy can say, “Oh, that guy is doing X, Y and Z wrong.” Your lawyer can talk about your agent: “Oh, in the c

item.title: When I give $1 million to Ferrari, I’m not giving $1 …
item.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo: Price is what you pay. Value is what you get. So in everything that I pay for — for me, how I operate my life — I want to see value. That’s why I buy art. I buy watches. I buy real estate. Everything that I pay for, everything that I put my money in, has to return value back to me. At the end of the day, when I look at $1 million, if you put $1 million in the market, by the end of the year, hopefully, if the market goes well — history has shown it always returns you 9 to 1

item.title: The other thing young athletes do is they focus on the …

People don’t understand: We make so much money while we play, and once you retire, the source of income won’t be the same. And they try to keep the same lifestyle. That cannot be possible. The other thing young athletes do is they focus on the chains. They focus on the cars. They focus so much on this false reality for lifestyle. Man, rich is what you see. Wealth is what you don’t se

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo: "That's not that dramatic. My …
item.description: "The stories are out there of sharing shoes with your brothers. Was it literally you would sub in and out of games and take shoes off, and that's the level of sharing the shoes it went?" Giannis Antetokounmpo: "That's not that dramatic. My game was from 6:00 to 8:00. His game was from 8:30 to 10:30. So when I was down playing, because I was younger than my brother three years, I'll come out, give him the shoes, he'll play, and then just vice versa."

item.title: "Sometimes in the locker room, when we go to like a …

It's time to let people know what you're about. So I usually don't dress up because when I go to the game, I just want to hoop. I really don't care how

And I said, okay, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, okay. And then when I got to the league, sure enough, I started to see the sharks and the piranhas."

item.title: Andre Drummond: "My GOAT is Michael Jordan,
item.description: "Who is your GOAT?" Andre Drummond: "My GOAT is Michael Jordan, by default. My mom is a super Michael Jordan fan, so that is who I grew up hearing about all day and all night."

They’re like how the Cowboys are loved, how the Yankees are loved, how the Lakers are loved. It’s a different type of love. Here’s an example: I came to a game a couple of years back, playoffs against Cleveland. And when I left, it was like I played and I had 30 in the Garden the night before, the fans were going crazy. I’m l

item.title: "I'm glad I'm not dealing with it all so [up] close," …
item.description: Larsa Pippen's son Preston got emotional as he opened up about his mom's alleged affair. During the fourth and sixth episodes of Netflix's Calabasas Confidential, the 23-year-old son of Larsa and her ex-husband Scottie Pippen spoke about his mom while with his friends. Preston mentioned that his mom was in a "weird place" and had a "new relationship per usual." "I'm glad I'm not dealing with it all so [up] close," said Preston. "It kind of sucks when your parents are divorced. That's one thing,

item.title: "Kids at school, like my friends, would play music by …
item.description: As he continued, Preston got emotional and said his parents don't speak with each other. "My parents got divorced when I was 15, and it was really tough because there was an affair," Preston said, referring to his mom Larsa's alleged affair with Future before officially separating from Scottie. "Kids at school, like my friends, would play music by a rapper that my mom was taking to at the time, and it was just s----y," he continued. "I am removed from it now, so it's easy to kind of talk about."

item.title: Clyde Drexler on Rick Adelman: 'Just an absolute gentleman, a prince of a guy'
item.description: He was inducted into the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame in 2021 and received the NBCA Chuck Daly Lifetime Achievement Award in 2023. “Just an absolute gentleman, a prince of a guy,” said Clyde Drexler, a Hall of Fame player who developed into an All-Star under Adelman in Portland. “I have nothing but respect and admiration for Rick Adelman. He was one of my favorite coaches of all time. And as good of a coach as he was, he was an even better person. Gone too soon.”

item.title: “He managed to have so much control with so few …
item.description: He wasn’t a rah-rah coach who delivered long motivational speeches or called his players out in the locker room. He was quiet by nature, so when he did speak, his words carried extra meaning. “He managed to have so much control with so few words,” Williams said. “It was amazing to me. He wasn’t a big speech-giver and he wasn’t the kind of guy who called you into his office to tell you a big story about life. He had a blueprint to follow, he put you in the right position to succeed, and he held y

item.title: Aspiration co-founder sentenced to 14 years in prison
item.description: A federal judge sentenced Aspiration co-founder Joseph Sanberg, whose now-bankrupt green banking company is at the center of an NBA investigation into the LA Clippers, to 14 years in federal prison Monday.

item.title: Mukasey instead advocated for a punishment that would …
item.description: Federal prosecutors sought a prison sentence of 212 months -- nearly 18 years -- while Sanberg's lead attorney, Marc Mukasey, argued that a prison sentence would be "too severe." Mukasey instead advocated for a punishment that would avoid any time behind bars for Sanberg, saying that Sanberg displayed "no malice, no ill will, no greed" in his actions and that he was not an outlaw but "a good person who did a bad thing." Judge Stephen V. Wilson, who has been a federal judge since 1985, disagreed.

item.title: In a federal courtroom in downtown Los Angeles, in …
item.description: In a federal courtroom in downtown Los Angeles, in front of people who said they had been defrauded by Aspiration and specifically by Sanberg, Wilson said the circumstances of Sanberg's actions were "among the worst I've ever seen" in his career on the bench before adding that "this case has touched almost every badge of fraud."

item.title: “I thought I was gone [from the NBA] bro, I ain’t …

To get an opportunity only in the G League and getting waived with no warning, no nothing, no explanation or anything was tough. “It was tough for a 22-year-old kid who was just thinking of chasing my dreams and telling myself I can do this. They’ve been scoring at a high clip, shooting the ball at a high percentage, everything just seems to be clicking right now. … I think if guys can stay healthy during this Finals run, I think we’ll have a real good shot at bringing the title home to New York.

This got back to Marty, who ripped me a new one for breaching the confidence of someone who was already wary of media. It

item.description: “When I came into the league (Wolves coach) Randy Wittman was like, ‘I just don’t see you shooting 3s right now,” Love said. “Maybe at some point you’ll step out and take a couple, but that’s not why we brought you here.’ It wasn’t really until Kurt Rambis, my third year, really pushed me into shooting 3s and kind of saw where the game was evolving and leading to.” “And for me, it started off by being trail 3s. I eventually got into the pick-and-pop. And that’s where, like, outside of Dirk, I wo

item.title: He went from making three 3s in 1987-88 to 82 in …
item.description: Sikma, like Gasol, was winning all the team’s shooting games in practice (Sikma was a career 84.9 percent foul shooter and led the league at 92.2 percent in 1987-88), so they finally realized he should shoot some of them in a game. He went from making three 3s in 1987-88 to 82 in 1988-89. The league’s other players listed as centers by Basketball-Reference that season made 88 3s … combined.

item.title: ”And then the post play changed when Shaq got in the …
item.description: Sikma also noted two big changes in the game since he played that made it more of a skill game than a power game. “Attacks off the dribble started to be more effective when they changed the hand-checking rules,” Sikma said. “That was brought on by Jordan. They wanted more space. ”And then the post play changed when Shaq got in the league and they changed how you could guard the post with one hand on the back and the other elbow bent. That made it harder to score in the post. Those two changes ma

item.title: “There’s two parts to it, your physical skills, how …
item.description: Sikma noted the combination of IQ and mobility that the modern game was asking from centers. Often it’s been the smart bigs — Gasol, Horford, Lopez — who held up the best. “There’s two parts to it, your physical skills, how well you can move, and then the second thing is how well you anticipate that the danger is over and it’s time to get your secondary responsibility,” he said.

item.title: According to our research, using a weighted average …
item.description: One of the reasons that not many thought San Antonio could make a deep run this season is because of the team's collective age. According to our research, using a weighted average that takes into account minutes played by each player, the Spurs are 25.15 years old on average, making them the youngest team since the 1976-77 Portland Trail Blazers to make it to the NBA Finals. Ironically enough, the team that San Antonio just edged out in this exercise is once again the Thunder, as the 2024-25 Okl

item.title: Those four players have combined for 1,778 of the …
item.description: Per our research, the Spurs boast an astonishing four players under the age of 23 who account for just south of 40 percent of their playoff minutes: Stephon Castle (21.6 years old), Wembanyama (22.4), Dylan Harper (20.2) and Carter Bryant (20.5). Those four players have combined for 1,778 of the team's 4,512 playoff minutes so far, i.e., 39.4 percent of the total. No Finals team in our database has leaned this heavily on players under 23.

item.title: Jeremy Lin — the Knicks guard who captivated the city …
item.description: Linsanity will be part of the NBA Finals. Jeremy Lin — the Knicks guard who captivated the city for about a month in February 2012 — is joining ESPN during his former team’s NBA Finals matchup with the Spurs, USA Today reported. His role will start Wednesday with Game 1 in San Antonio.

item.title: Alex Caruso going nowhere?
item.description: Caruso, entering the second season of a four-year, $81 million extension, is considered a firm member of the long-term core, league sources said, proving again his extreme playoff value the past month in the locker room and on the court. Hartenstein and Dort also remained beloved organizationally, but their team options demand a more urgent decision -- opt them into an expiring, opt them out, trade the number at a positive market value or work out a decline-and-extend at a lower starting number.

item.title: OKC keeping Isaiah Hartenstein?
item.description: There is a strong anticipation among team and league sources that Hartenstein will be back in Oklahoma City next season, whether the Thunder choose to pick up his team option or the sides agree to an extension. With Wembanyama and the Spurs looming as a long-term rival, it would be a significant step in the wrong direction for the Thunder to thin out their big man depth, especially after Hartenstein had some success defending Wembanyama during the West finals.

item.title: While Gilgeous-Alexander publicly vowed not to provide …
item.description: The decision on Dort is considered murkier, in part due to Wallace's presence as a potential replacement in the starting lineup who is in line for a significant raise, starting in the 2027-28 season. While Gilgeous-Alexander publicly vowed not to provide input to Presti on personnel matters, sources familiar with the superstar's thinking said that he privately intends to emphasize to management how much he values playing alongside Canadian countryman Dort. Dort switched agents from Thaddeus Fouc

item.title: The most likely candidates to be moved in a trade to …
item.description: The most likely candidates to be moved in a trade to rebalance the roster are Isaiah Joe and Aaron Wiggins, who have proved to be quality role players but fell out of the rotation during these playoffs. Joe is due $11.3 million next season and has a team option at the same salary for 2027-28. Wiggins is due $9.2 million next season and $8.3 million in 2027-28 with an $8.3 million team option in 2028-29.

item.title: Rival executives anticipate that the Thunder will …
item.description: Kenrich Williams, another quality role player who has been in and out of Oklahoma City's deep rotation, has a team option for $7.2 next season. Rival executives anticipate that the Thunder will decline that option, although Williams could return at a reduced salary.

item.title: “If there were little changes that I thought were …
item.description: It’s clear from our conversation that Dundon was caught off-guard by the criticism over his early decisions in Portland, which included replacing free T-shirts for fans at home playoff games with towels and, in an unprecedented-for-the-NBA move, not traveling with its two-way players to San Antonio for the start of the first round. (Dundon later apologized for the latter.) But, in reflecting on what he would do differently, he only expressed regret about the timing. “If there were little changes

item.title: Blazers owner Tom Dundon: 'I don’t make the decisions to save money. I make the decisions to win'
item.description: Overall, continued Dundon, whose net worth is estimated at $2.3 billion by Forbes, any perceived belt-tightening is really driven by another simple goal: “I don’t make the decisions to save money. I make the decisions to win.”

item.title: Dundon does not apologize. “We kept a lot of people …
item.description: The cuts also stoked fears among fans that the franchise would eventually be relocated. But again, Dundon does not apologize. “We kept a lot of people and got rid of a lot of people and all the story was about who you got rid off, not who you kept,” he said. “I think the ability to look at everybody and make sure the people you absolutely have to have (ultimately) stay with you is important. And we did it here.”

item.title: “I give him tons of credit,” coach Rod Brind’Amour …
item.description: Eight years ago, the Dallas-based Dundon, who made his fortune in subprime auto lending, bought a downtrodden NHL franchise in Carolina and swiftly put his stamp on its operations. Now, after the league’s first 12-1 start to a postseason in 50 years, the Hurricanes are four wins away from their second Stanley Cup championship, with Game 1 against the Vegas Golden Knights set for Tuesday night. “I give him tons of credit,” coach Rod Brind’Amour said of Dundon. “We’ve been pretty solid ever since

item.title: “The fact of the matter is, he doesn’t always do …
item.description: To many who have worked with Dundon in hockey, whether on the Hurricanes or from the vantage point of the NHL’s league office, this recent avalanche of criticism from the basketball world has come as a surprise. In speaking for this story, they provided many colorful words to describe a man laser-focused on both winning games and eliminating inefficiencies — including “demanding,” “intense,” “data-driven” and “obsessive.” But “cheap” was not something any of them had experienced. “The fact of th

item.title: Anonymous NBA scout picks Spurs over Knicks
item.description: Minutes after the Spurs' Game 7 victory in Oklahoma City, ESPN's Tim Bontemps spoke with a veteran NBA scout on the keys to the Finals: "I think I still give the Spurs the advantage, but there's a nagging thing from the end of this game: With all the emotion, it immediately hit me that they may have a hangover coming out of this series. The Knicks have the problem of being off for a long time, so you can say that's its own issue, but it just seems San Antonio put so much into this series ... hav

item.title: That is only the greatest 11-game stretch any team has …
item.description: Now, led by Brunson -- who has already placed himself on the Mount Rushmore of all-time great Knicks alongside those three stars listed above -- New York comes roaring into the Finals having ripped off 11 straight victories with a point differential of plus-262. That is only the greatest 11-game stretch any team has played in the 80 years the NBA has existed, covering both regular and postseason play.

item.title: Remarkably, their offensive efficiency was the highest …
item.description: The Knicks just finished smoking the Eastern Conference competition, posting a 123.3 offensive rating on their way to the NBA Finals. Remarkably, their offensive efficiency was the highest intraconference mark posted by an East champion since the league's current playoff format was instituted in 2003, supplanting LeBron James' 2017 Cleveland Cavaliers.

item.title: While the Spurs ranked third in defensive efficiency …
item.description: Get this: The Knicks also posted a blistering 123.0 offensive rating in their two regular-season games against San Antonio, and they torched the Spurs in the NBA Cup championship (which doesn't officially count in the standings). While the Spurs ranked third in defensive efficiency during the regular season, they conceded more points per possession to the Knicks than any of their opponents. This Finals will be decided by San Antonio's ability -- or inability -- to slow the New York juggernaut. T

item.title: “(If you could) go into a lab and create…the physical …
item.description: Ian Begley: “(If you could) go into a lab and create…the physical profile you need to have a chance against him, it probably would look a lot like OG Anunoby.” On NBA Finals on ABC conference call, analysts Tim Legler and Richard Jefferson were asked about OG Anunoby potentially defending Victor Wembanyama. Legler and Jefferson's full thoughts on Anunoby defending Wembayama: Legler: “Just looking at Wemby all year and sort of the evolution of his career…if you could have a physical profile that

item.title: "But for me, OG Anunoby - even the way that he's …
item.description: "But for me, OG Anunoby - even the way that he's guarded him this year - is the type of defender that you have to have (in order to have) a chance. Because of (Anunoby’s) combination of just agility laterally, which you need - length to contest as much as you can from a guy that's 7-5 with a high release point, and then the physical strength and determination. You have to have a guy that is so committed to that end of the floor; and in this case, it's unique because (Anunoby) isn't a guy who's a

item.title: "So it's a major task for him and the minutes he's on …
item.description: "So it's a major task for him and the minutes he's on the court; he'll be the primary guy on him. And I think in this situation the Knicks are probably going to do what they can to kind of keep him on (Wembanyama) as much as they can. Certainly, sometimes you have to switch or there’s a cross match. but he is the guy that they would want to have. I think they've got about as good a defender as you can for this type of matchup/responsibility.”

item.title: Would agree…. And understand, there is no stopping …
item.description: JEFFERSON: "I would agree…. And understand, there is no stopping players in the NBA. That's not a real thing. I told a story a little bit ago about Kobe Bryant; (he) said Tony Allen was the best player to ever guard him. And Kobe averaged 29 (points per game) on Tony Allen. So while OG is a perfect player, in my opinion, to guard Wemby, the size, the consistency, the competitiveness in which he brings, he's still giving up a foot. So it's one of those things that, it's the physicality aspect, an

item.title: "We are building a system that will benefit the …
item.description: NBA deputy commissioner Mark Tatum provided another update on the NBA Europe project, confirming the league's target launch timeline. According to Tatum, NBA Europe is expected to begin in October 2027. He also said the NBA is prepared to move forward with the project alongside FIBA and its partners if an agreement with EuroLeague is not reached, though discussions between the two sides remain ongoing. "We are building a system that will benefit the European clubs starting from October 2027," Ta

item.description: Benjamin Royer: Lakers announced the hiring of Yao Williams II as Vice President and Head of Global Partnerships. Williams has worked for the Yankees, Manchester City, the NBA, and most recently, was the VP of Global Partnerships for Elevate.

item.description: NBA on ESPN: "[Victor Wembanyama is going] to make you question everything you've even learned about the game." -- Chris Paul shares his thoughts on Wemby's impact on the court

item.description: Ian Begley: Victor Wembanyama on Knicks matchup: “It’s a great team of experienced guys who are not here by chance…. They got here (through) relentless effort… they’re right where they’re supposed to be.”
